review_text: Love the location, always stay here when visiting Chester. Lovely hotel, leisure facilities, great breakfast, excellent evening meal and fantastic staff. No complaints!
Decent breakfast cooked to order.  Dinner was OK but on the pricey side. Parking was not connected to but in sight of the hotel.  As I was on a motorcycle, it did not feel secure.
Location cleanliness value for money good staff good food and service There was no drawers ,no hooks to hang your coats, not enough coat hangers,no mirror or plugs  near mirrors to dry your hair

Breakfast was amazing…..  Good choice and all appeared fresh….  Just no orange juice….  Good location for Chester  Lovely friendly staff. The shower was a bit hit and miss….  Water very very hot…..  took some time to work out.   Bedding very clean… nice fresh towels.     Shame we had to pay extra for parking….. would have been nice to be included in price.
Good location.excellent spa facilities, excellent breakfast,great bar drinks and staff N.a
Friendly staff grate location and good bar. No information about breckfast had to find it on website.

excellent value for money,  staff were really good and helpful, food in the restaurant was to a high standard, the spa area was really good as well kids loved the pool. the breakfast could do with some things being grilled, everything was fried, and the room could have been hoovered better, pool would have been better a bit warmer for the little ones, but that’s being very fussy.

Central location.  Good food.  Some staff members were very friendly. We had to pay £18 to park in the car park. We’ve never paid before or at any other hotels! Also the rooms were extremely hot. The hotel should consider installing air con.

Great location near town centre. Good secure car parking (fee to pay). Big well appointed room. Lovely hot breakfast. Swimming pool and spa! Car parking fee if don't book direct with hotel but worth paying for secure parking and near town centre. We went on a Monday but can see if want a Saturday/weekend have to book 2 nights which is fair enough

Excellent breakfast although coffee could be better.   Cheery staff making a good start to the day   Position of hotel by the canal made a pleasant walk into the city . Having asked about disabled parking I was surprised we were put in a room which couldn’t have benn further from reception!

The hotel was lovely just a little bit dated. However it was nice and clean, rooms are big, shower was fab and staff were very helpful and friendly.   Breakfast was spot on. Spa was lovely too. Had to ring reception in the middle of the night as we were woke but the room above. Sounded like a child was running around and jumping.   This was sorted immediately by staff and we had a restful nights sleep.

Brilliant stay staff very friendly and welcoming bedroom very clean and comfortable food was lovely and a very relaxing atmosphere and location was great just a few minutes walk from city centre Nothing at all
Such a nice place and in a great area. Staff were brilliant and the room was perfectly clean, very comfy bed. leaving
A good location close to the city centre and by the canal There was nothing really to complain about

We have stayed at the Mill many times and will continue to do so.  The hotel is in the centre of Chester by the canal and has its own private car park where you can park your car safely for the duration of your stay.  We had a standard room which was a bit on the small side to be honest but was perfectly comfortable, lovely comfy bed, excellent facilities, private bathroom with bath and shower.  It is only a short walk to the Centre of Chester of about 10 minutes and the City Walls walk is slightly less.    There is free use of the leisure facilities for hotel guests including a good size swimming pool, jacuzzi, steam room and sauna.   Breakfast is served buffet style and is excellent, the meal we had in the restaurant was also excellent. The lighting in the room is disappointing in that the only lighting in the room is spot lights above the bed which run along the length of the room and there are no bedside lamps.  If you need to get up in the night both sides of the bed are flooded with light and there is no provision for one person to read while the other sleeps as their is no individual lighting.  Bedside lamps would alleviate this.
It was conveniently located, clean and comfortable We didn’t have any hot water for the last day

room was very clean and spacious, staff very helpful and pleasant. good choice for breakfast and plenty of it. good location only a 5 min walk into centre our room overlooked the main road so there was a little bit of noise as cars passed

This was our second stay in the space of a fortnight and we were very happy with the comfort, service and location of the hotel On this occasion our room was next to one of the housekeeping stations where all of the equipment is kept and the team members gather. There was a lot of noise and when we left our room we had to wade through piles of bedding sheets waiting to be laundered.
Excellent breakfast. Location perfect. Parking perfect. Staff so lovely Nothing
Breakfast room superb and bar clean and friendly nan

Wonderful re-purposing of a historic Chester industrial building along the canal. Very nicely done interiors. Great gym, pool, work-out area. City center location, five minute walk to historical sights or to rail station. Generous breakfast. Comfortable bed and pillows. Just sorry did not have time to book into the spa!
Located very close to the town centre, facilities were excellent as well as the staff were extremely friendly/helpful and nothing was out of their way. Could have done with some air conditioning
Lovely hotel.Staff very helpful.great location.Great breakfast BedRoom a little dated.No convenient plug for kettle.Had to carry boiled kettle across room
The location on the canal and easy walking distance to the city centre. Breakfast and the spa facilities were excellent. Friendly staff Nothing to dislike
position very central friendly staff excellent meals no safe no water in room

The staff were all pleasant and helpful. Had a small problem with the plug in the bathroom. After being unable to get housekeeping to come up one of the Receptionists came and fixed it. Which I thought went beyond the call of duty. The room was very nice - quite luxurious in decor. Spacious bathroom and shower easy to use. The room was quiet and comfortable. There are pool and gym facilities but did not use them. Breakfast was good with very pleasant and friendly staff. And an easy stroll into the historic centre of Chester. All in all a very pleasant stay. You have to pay for the car park on top of the hotel charge. This was £9.00, which is reasonable for a city centre. My only niggle is that we did not know how to get into the car park until we had booked in and paid, which meant we had to park on the street. However we were very lucky in that we found a temporary parking space. Perhaps they could give an option to pay for parking and get the code before arrival on Booking.com?
Efficient check in procedure. After 3 weeks on a small canal boat the spacious room at this hotel was most welcome. Excellent location with easy walk to centre of the city nothing!
Location was excellent short walk to town centre and bars nice clean friendly hotel spa was great too Nothing
Price and location Nothing
Spacious hotel rooms good bar and leisure facilities close to city centre Husband missed mushrooms at breakfast
Great place good value Bit strange to find from station

We didn't use the facilities but they looked really good. Room was huge with comfortable bed. Free car park. Welcoming staff Very noisy from the road ( we were over the bridge). Would have been nice to serve own breakfast. Old dated furniture in bedroom but can't complain at the price.
Location perfect !! Breakfast great !! Spa great !! Good 💯 😀 nan

Great location,room was very comfortable,great staff very helpful and welcoming,the food in the restaurant was of a high quality and value for money,all in all we had a lovely weekend and will definitely stay again Nothing that we could see

Perfect location for city centre.  Hotel clean comfortable.  Breakfast was nice. Small shower curtain over bath, didn't reach all the way round, floor got soaked.  Would like mushrooms on breakfast buffet, also no orange juice.
Room cleN tidy and spacious Nothing

Really good location easy to walk to everything we wanted to see. Room was a little dated however I am aware the hotel is updating and considering it was a room that still needed doing it was still nice. The pool is nice and hot tub. We didn't use the spa facilities fir any treatments or gym but they looked really good. The hotel resturant is lovely. I have stayed before and each time have eaten an evening meal at the hotel it doesn't disappoint.  The staff are friendly and helpful. I have a spinal injury and find the beds here are firm and comfortable. Like all hotels the walls can be a little thin. We had what sounded like a family of elephants above us so I recommend taking ear plugs. There was an issue on this stay with a previous guest hiding something foul. Luckily the hotel responded very quickly and had it all sorted quickly.
Great location. Great breakfast. Staff lovely Room and bathroom a little tired
The location is great the hotel is nice but I think abit over priced for the room. Needs more tables outside when it's nice weather.

All round excellent stay at the Mill Hotel. Great facilities and wonderful staff. Excellent room service keeps the Hotel exceptionally clean. Nothing is too much trouble, staff go to Great lengths to make your stay, one to remember. Swimming pool sauna, jacuzzi and Spa are brilliant. We'll done to the Mill. nan
Warm room,bar,pool Room right at the back of the building

There was plenty of choice for breakfast. The hotel is a short walk from the city walls an centre Our room was on the South West, over the canal bridge which would have been fine if it was facing the canal but unfortunately it was on the other side of the corridor right next to the motorway so quite a bit of noise from the traffic sadly
Breakfast was excellent & staff service was also very good. Nothing really
Room was perhaps a little dated, but clean and comfortable and breakfast was lovely Paying to park and the parking availability

The hotel facilities were really good and staff were very helpful and polite, the location was excellent, the breakfast was delicious. I feel as though the rooms could do with a little update. They were very clean but a bit dated. Other than that, we really enjoyed our stay and will be coming back.
Friendly staff , room was clean and warm  Breakfast was good too Bed wasn't the most comfy ...but was OK

Everything at The Mill was superb.  The staff are so friendly and nothing is too much trouble for them.  Food is excellent, great choice for breakfast, lunch and dinner. Spa facilities are so relaxing. nan
Cleanliness. Location. Breafast very good. Menu in restaurant quite limited.

The location was excellent just a short walk to the centre and on the side of the canal. Great breakfast shame there was no mushrooms. Though the hotel sent information about party nights at the hotel. Little information about their restaurant. Would have used it one night. Food looked good
Great value and comfortable rooms. We enjoyed the breakfast too. Nothing
Comfortable bed,pleasant staff. Mediocre breakfast. Poor quality bathroom accessories.

The rooms were comfortable. Nice size. Quiet location. Pool and changing rooms were clean. Staff were exceptional. Polite and eager to help in any way. Breakfast was nicely laid out, fresh and good quality. nan
clean and close location to town centre lovely hotel to stay in
the staff were all so nice polite professional and friendly from reception to housekeeping to spa staff and breakfast staff. room was spacious and clean. nan

Very little negative to say.  A great stay with good facilities and and nice breakfast.  The hotel, although not all new/modern, was very clean and comfortable.  The biggest positive was the staff - all staff we interacted with were happy and very helpful, they all seemed happy to be working there. Only negative I can make is that the mattresses in our family room, although not uncomfortable, were not among the best.
Excellent location, Breakfast very well controlled, Staff Excellent. Room had no drawer space .Tired furniture

Great location, parking available, pool, gym, salon, food, very friendly staff too. Just one thing that would have been good was if the rooms had air conditioning - it was pretty hot on a warm weekend but the hotel is an old mill building so it's probably not possible to install air con throughout.
Cleanliness, food quality, bar facilities and Spa The rooms need Aircon and a fridge
Popular place to stay. Helpful staff. Enjoyed breakfast. Car parking just across road. Shower in room had a mixer problem & ran cold. Staff tried but couldn't fix that evening.
Breakfast and location very good and met our expectations All good
Very friendly, great location for City centre. Clean rooms and comfortable beds. nan
clean and location is good nice spa as well and the breakfast you won’t be disappointed nothing
Convenient for Chester. Room had everything we needed. Great value for money. Nothing

Everything was perfect other than the rooms. The pool was lovely, breakfast was great and the canal was beautiful. , the rooms could use a little updating, I was opening the blinds and the rail fell down on me. They just need a little revamp and a little more maintaining.

It's my third stay at the mill spa but this time we had a massage, it was a bitter sweet stay as it was my dad's 1st anniversary of his sad demise. I thought the woman on reception was a bit arrogant over the car park charges having to pay when we had just arrived 😳 instead of check out.
Great Hotel, fantastic location. The spa and swimming facility were relaxing. Thought the parking could of been included in the price. Nothing apart from the parking cost.
Great location with an even better spa and pool Nothing to dislike
Staff were lovely ,food was delicious and location great nan
Brill breakfast Car park should be included
The location is excellent, car park outside a plus in Chester, breakfast is hot fresh and tasty More hooks 8n the room for coats etc.

Hotel location was excellent facilities were of a good standard  Staff were friendly and hardworking and polite and helpful. Breakfast was well presented and portions were substantial.  Enjoyed our stay. Nothing.
location is fantastic, large car park, spacious rooms and really helpful staff nothing
Everything, spa brilliant,  breakfast very good . Location bang on. Also staff very helpful, I'll be back again in September. 👍 Nothing to dislike.
The room was good apart from been on road side and traffic noise kept us awake will return in the future will request canal side Road side room
Had a quiet room, as requested. Well placed for station, historic city and eating out nan

Location. Room size Aircon not working and reception unable to resolve issue, rooms very warm as windows don't open. Card only payment at bar, no notice to say cash not accepted.  Breakfast not open until 7am is not an issue, but when you arrive at 06:50 and you are not allowed to enter because its not 7!! The place was ready to go and all staff standing waiting for the clock to strike.
clean fresh and very quiet bedrooms too hot but that’s personal preference
comfortable , clean nan

The room (especially the bathroom) was lovely, super clean and the coffee/tea making facilities were excellent. The gym was great considering it's a city centre hotel, on site parking was really useful (if a little expensive). The aircon in the room wasn't great - it switched on and off with seemingly no logic so the room could feel a little stuffy at times. Easily fixed by switching it back on. My room also didn't have a deadbolt on the inside of the door.
very clean and all facilities were up to a good standard nothing

Great hotel in the center of Birmingham, with clean rooms and bathrooms. The check-in and check-out process was quick. The breakfast was very good, although it could benefit from more diversity. The service is good, and has good value for money compared to nearby 4-star hotels in the center Small issue that one of the servant don’t know English, difficult to communicate with her and sometime enter the room without permission.
Staff very nice and friendly. Room was very clean and comfortable. Bathroom was lovely. Loved the freebies. Could have some more lifts as had to wait ages to go up and down.

Super breakfast and good service.Large , clean room.Good location. Sleep disturbances due to other inconsiderate guests coming in and continuing to party after a night out in Birmingham.Hotel did their best to resolve the situation.
Everything was excellent lovely comfortable bed breakfast was delicious Nothing
Good location, lots of dining options nearby  Beautiful bathroom  Everyday cleaning is good No
Nice comfortable big bed lovely bathroom and very clean room Nothing
The extra charges for things like room service or parking are extortionate Room service charges
We had 2 rooms, both were very nice and clean, the beds were quite firm. Breakfast was delicious. We were only there one night and in that time there was nothing to dislike.
Location is perfect , Hotel Rooms are spacious, Breakfast amazing Nothing
Deluxe room, perfect for a romantic weekend with a partner.  Very good facilities and general cleanliness. nan
Breakfast was amazing as was the staff apart from reception.  Great showers and quiet rooms Check in could have been alot better
Comfortable room, nice bar, great location the breakfast coffee was terrible.
Very nice hotel parking onsite, beautiful room and facilities,  great choice of breakfast. Location was great Nothing all good
Excellent rooms larger than average, beds very comfortable nan
Room, bathroom all lovely. Not sure why I always get given a room with 2 double beds? Expensive and no breakfast included. Otherwise everything really good.
Comfortable, clean, convenient Cupboard stunk of bananas. interesting
Excellence place place and beutiful accommodation for our family Nil
Clean and fabulous service, everyone friendly nan

Excellent location. Modern design, attractive lobby area, a very clean and comfortable room. A few little details at reception and in the room that don't cost much but made a big difference in terms of comfort. Just the noise from the street (clubs below), and how long we had to wait for a lift to go down each time, but nothing major.
Everything. Location was amazing, room was very clean & nicely presented upon arrival. Room service menu was great. Customer service was amazing. Buffet breakfast was my favourite. Nothing.
Very clean, comfortable beds, good location. No soundproofing between bedrooms.  Bring earplugs!

Great location, facilities,reception staff, on site parking , excellent breakfast,  and great WiFi. Very quiet room Very abrupt lady on omelette stand shouting at customers going the wrong way on buffet stand ( signs would be useful) and for putting utensils in wrong place. She made customers feel and look stupid.  Even after I apologised for not being aware of system she kept ranting at me. Also did this with overseas guests who were not fluent in English. Otherwise would have given you a 10!
Great location lovely room and comfy bed Breakfast price for to high
Clean, good location, Cleaning staff turning up at 8.30 to do the room when check out is 11 am
Hotel is very clean, staff are very good and location was perfect for us... A better selection of drinks in hotel bar would have been perfect...

great hotel in good location and great values for money when we phoned we were told there was no car park but after parking elsewhere were told on check in that there was a car park which would have been cheaper. Very irritating

Lovely staff member at reception, got a free room upgrade which was amazing with great views of the canal! Bathroom was stunning😍 food in resturant great, amazing location busy street with clubs/bars couldnt hear any noise in room at night, only 10 min walk from city centre, amazing stay N/a
Fantastic location for the arena. Very comfortable room, two double beds so a very restful sleep. Recommend the breakfast, something for every taste. Nothing.

Location is very good for nightlife Room was brand new. Very clean and modern. Room comes with a fridge and coffee pod machine The noise from the street party outside, we stayed on the 2nd floor next time i would request a room on a higher floor
Perfect location, room was spacious & clean Housekeeping entered the room before we checked out
Location was very good.When I got my bearings.The breakfast was nice.The staff were really helpful. Nothing it was all acceptable,it was what expected really.
I liked  everything about the hotel it's  in a great  location room  was fabulous  staf  very  nice a really  lovely place to stay Nothing
That is where i wanted to be central birmigham to enjoy night life it was perfect locations for us 👌 Everythings
Room was spotless, spacious, bed very comfortable, extra pillows, top marks Nothing it was perfect
the size of bed nothing

Wonderfully comfortable. The beds were superb. The rooms were spotless and the little touches, eg coffee, tea etc of a high quality. The bathroom extremely clean, with a fabulous power shower. Nothing. An excellent hotel
Room was great with a lovely big shower and the bed was very comfy. Check in was slow and only one member of staff in the morning at check out, who was busy helping a couple.
Pleasant rooms, excellent bathroom, welcoming reception and lounge area. Nothing.
Everything about the hotel was perfect especially the beds and the breakfast nan

The room was large with a huge comfortable bed. There was a fridge, bottle of water, plenty of tea bags plus a coffee machine even a couple of welcome cup cakes. The shower was spacious and powerful. There wasn't a huge choice of restaurants in close proximity to the hotel but there were numerous options at the 'Mailbox' area about a 10 minute walk away.

Smart hotel, spacious room and good breakfast. Had a Tai curry in the bar that was inedible as it was way way too hot, and I am a curry eater, but it was ridiculous. Could not, would not refund but was offered a free drink. Lack of veg food. Toilet flush was not working properly. Look que for check in as only one person on duty.
great location, clean and well decorated, great bed and friendly welcoming staff i only stayed one night so didn’t find anything wrong at all

Central Hotel with great facilities. Breakfast was great and lots of variety. Quite difficult to get to. On a one way street, and sat nav took us along a bus and taxi zone. Couldn't see any other way to getting to the hotel. Toilet didn't flush well at all.
Very clean and modern. Rooms are a good size and beds are comfy. Staff were very helpful and friendly. nan

Large room, two comfortable double beds, clean, room was quiet, felt safe - my daughter and I stayed at the hotel, we arrived back late and I like the fact that the hotel only let people in that we’re staying at the hotel. nothing
very clean excellent facilities and location. large comfortable room nan
Rooms are lovely,air con fab as it was a nice evening so more comfortable getting ready to go out.   Feel safe as people are on the door at night letting guests back in. nan

Fab location, lovely rooms and staff The breakfast and parking. For the price you paid the breakfast was not the best, it was cold. For the price of the room you would expect parking to be included or at least discounted
Great beds and breakfast nan

Well located, lots of restaurants nearby & easy transport links- bus, tram,taxis to hand. Comfortable rooms with tea, coffee, biscuits & water, lovely bathrooms, huge lobby with plenty of seating areas. Good breakfast. To reach the parking garage you go through the lower ground floor which is a little rough looking. The garage itself isn’t too bad. More vegan options needed for breakfast- why not offer vegan alternatives, porridge etc?
Excellent room and breakfast and very polite staff nan

good location very clean and friendly staff the cost of the car park £16.50p on top of coast of the room, I booked my room which was the better executive room for £125 and my friend who stayed in an identical room for £80 and we both booked with booking.com, I wasn’t happy with this at all
Location Room facilities & comfort Modern feel throughout hotel nan
Comfy beds, lovely bathroom, fab shower. Good breakfast & facilities. nan
the hotel is clean and in a very central location.  rooms are spacious. nan
Perfect location  Modern rooms Plenty of room for family of 4 Cost of parking

the room was ready early which helped fit into our plans. the room was clean, tidy and bed really comfortable. breakfast was average but plenty available with large selection. nothing really .. we was going to eat in restaurant at night but not great selection and it was rather pricey
Clean and very comfortable room and the hotel is in a great location. nan
big comfy beds, luxurious bathroom, great choice of coffees, big tv, cosy room. nan
comfy beds and very clean room check in was not good queue was in the street took me 45 minutes to check in
Great price, convenient location The rooms were extremely small, which I expected, however not much room for luggage. It is manageable definitely not for long term stay.
How friendly the front staff were. The shuttle bus was great as we arrived there at 2am. The smallness of the floor space however it was very clean and comfortable
Good location,friendly staff and very clean. nan
so easy to get to from airport plus food next door everything good
Very good location, we could walk with our luggage from the international airport to the hotel. Bed took up almost all the room
The location and comfort of the room was fine. Nothing really, expected the room to be smallish since it is at the airport.
The room was quiet and the property was easily accessible from the airport. nan

the room was pretty basic and small but then it was budget hotel. Nothing really but for the fact with 2 large cases because we'd been travelling for 4 weeks it was hard to find anywhere to put them without falling over them.
Walking distance to the airport. Close to places to eat. The bunk beds only had foam mattresses that were not that comfortable.
Rooms were very spacious and comfortable. It also had wifi nan
Handy to airport  bus airport to front  door   room s,mall but all you need one  night stop over   half the price of novetal Got what u paidfor
Location bed Small room
Close to airport and supermarket; cooking and eating area good Needed more crockery, cutlery and another microwave would be good

Staff were friendly.  Super close to the airport.  They have a cot for a baby.  Love that there is a coffee station in the morning. The room I stayed in would be perfect for a small family or friends traveling. Is there any way to speed up the check in process,  arriving at 2.30 and having to wait 20 minutes to check in with a baby is rough,  but your one staff did it quickly.  There were a couple of hairs in the bathroom and in the bed.
Great location. Staff were friendly and aloud us to have a late checkout due to arriving late /on a 12.30am flight. nan
Clean. Tidy, excellent shower, good location and facilities close nan

Not as spartan as Ibis Budget overseas. Net CE to have an airport hotel at the airport although a bit far to walk. Hot water jugs and peppermint tea a nice touch. At the airport, too close for a cab but quite a hike nonetheless. Front desk a bit abrupt after a long international flight.
I got the bus from the airport because it was drizzling and I wasn't sure exactly where you were.  Sunny in the morning so I waked to the airport to catch my next flight. Nothing
Location, price,restaurant next door nan

I've stayed here previously and will again. It's a perfect budget airport transit hotel. Clean and comfortable with a good bar next door and plenty of fast food joints close by if that's your thing. Airport shuttles should be free.

I liked that the staff were very friendly. The room clean and security use of card to use elevator was great. Excellent facilities perhaps a kiosk to buy food for travellers be awesome.Snacks Hot meal to heat up in a microwave. I didnt like to leave on a cold morning.
Location to airport is great……and excellent bar/restaurant next door. N/A

Good location. From the airport take the Yellow bus (bus stop between domestic airport gate 2 and 3) and the first stop is the Ibis. Alternatively if you don’t have much luggage it is walkable. Location ideal for a night in Auckland prior to flying out early next morning. Free espresso coffee from a machine in the common area on the ground floor.
near airport small cramped room for family with lots of luggage
closeness to airport, had a very late check in and receptionist allowed me a late check out. room very clean! no plugs close to the bed

Location to the airport and the free parking I find it unbelievable that a hotel of this size has windows that do not open.   I get that its a safety feature given the height of the building, however to not have the ability to allow fresh air to flow through a hotel surprises me - even putting hinges on the windows would have been an option when that Hotel was built I would have thought!  I do realise however that this feature will not ever change.      I had to sleep on the bottom bunk and the bunks may well have been placed in the rooms with the intention that children would sleep on them but a non plastic mattress would be preferable.  Mattresses on the bunks are not comfortable at all for adults and I would not recommend, unfortunately I did not have a choice given who I was sharing the room with.
Close to airport and next to Countdown shop, the room was perfect for an Airport stopover Rooms are a bit small but fine for a short one night stay.

Staff were really friendly and helpful especially at 5.30am. Location perfect as I jad an early flight next morning and what was available within walking distance, supermarket, takeaways, restaurant, pharmacy. Very minor, didn't have a power point by my bed.

Walk from domestic airport terminal, near supermarket.  Kept my bags for the day I left so I could walk back to International Terminal. Near bus stop for city. Noise in corridor but hotel does ask people to be quiet.

It was a great location for a stop over. Close to airport. Very comfortable bed for sleeping which is what we needed to do. Great if that's all you need. Room size if staying long term but it suits its purpose.

Easy to walk to from airport! Little room but we knew that so not unexpected. Perfect for a late night arrival and early departure. Bed very comfy. Very clean and tidy. Was a little bit too warm in the room. Couldn’t seem to adjust temperature.
Basic but clean and all you need for a night close to airport. Ideal location easy to walk to airport Nothing
Close to the Airport, and 24/7 front desk.  Comfy Very small rooms, but what you would expect for a budget accomodation

Clean room, clean facilities, Annoying airconditioner, it randomly blowing  noisy air. Noisy corridor, there are guest chatting in the hallway having big laughs and conversation, we did not have a pleasant sleep.

Great location  and handy to all food facilities you could wish for . right beside airport and no noise! so no need for long travels in car means more time to rest up. The attached restaurant  was not ready to  open at 7am  for breakfast like it advertised. The " cleaner" wanted to bring us in through a side door despite no "chef" there  to prepare breakfast, We declined and walked away. The other person that was with us had cold soup the night before and it twenty minutes to heat it up!  He was upset! two times .  Not good  to be associated so attached to IBIS budget!
vicinity  price all fine
Good for airport travel Room was rather small.
Very close to the airport. Quiet room Small room but served the purpose we required. Staff weren’t very friendly
Location is perfect, beds comfortable and the rooms are very clean Nothing
The community hub was fantastic. We bought microwavable dinners at the nearby Countdown and used the microwave to heat up our dinner and eat there with the cutlery and crockery provided. Nothing

Close to the airport, the yellow busses work really well. Good value for money and the rooms have got everything you need. Great if you need only a night or so in between flights, staff were friendly and everything was clean. nan

The accommodation is close to the airport. I stayed there two times and walked both times to the terminal. It's minimalistic, so all you need before a flight. I stayed there two times and had different rooms. The first one with a single queen size bed was better. The second room with two single bed was really small, so I had problems opening my suitcase on the floor.

The hotel was close to the airport, food and shops. It was clean, comfy and had everything we needed for a quick stop over. The rooms weren’t connecting. We’d booked 2 rooms as we traveled as a family of 6. It would of been more convenient if the rooms had a joining door available to avoid the in and out through the rooms
Location. And price. Too small.
Walking distance from domestic and international airports. Very clean. Everything you need from an airport hotel. nan
Close to international Airport and also very quiet.  I gòt an excellent rest. nan
Helpful staff, convenience for airport nan

It is very close to the airport, also a lovely shopping centre if you need to fill in time. The free coffee in the lobby was very nice. The pillow was so high and hard, I hardly had any sleep. The room was tiny.
Close to airport and cheap Nothing much to dislike ....you get what you pay for.
Neat and clean room Comfort  Bed space Transport services to Haram

The location is super good. Hardly 10-12 minutes of walk to Al Haram. Excellent bus services which is sometimes ruined by the anxious guests but hotel services is not be blamed for this. I was hosted and greeted by ‘Dawood & Jalis” at tower 1 floor 28, these guys were super helpful and welcoming Hotel isn’t brand new, its quite old

ROOM WAS CLEAN AND SILENT. VERY GOOD LOCATION. THE ONLY THING I EXPERIENCED WAS LATE CHECK IN AS I ARRIVED EARLY MORNING 4 AM AND I ALREADY REQUESTED FOR EARLY CHECK IN AND AS PER THE CHAT IT WAS APPROVED. BUT UPON ARRIVING I WAS CHARGED FOR ADDITIONAL NIGHT. the shuttle buses should be changed. because the buses used for shuttle are coaches and steps r high. its difficult for old people.

The hotel was very clean room service was good like a 5*,  the shuttle service was very convenient every 20 mins to haram, small 8 mins walk from the bus stop to haram, good hotel if you have young single companions not recommended for family children & old age guests. We had 6 rooms booked the  staff checked the wrong room out, we had  guests locked out of the room with luggage inside for 2 hours.
Good hotel and value for money. Clean and tidy. nan
big room clean  close to Al Haram bad Wi-Fi poor breakfast
very well behaved staffs, shuttle bus service was very convenient & value for money location and pocket friendliness

The lady checked the electronic planner and said they weren’t busy, so we could just turn up. The following evening we turned up and was told by the same lady that we should have booked and they couldn’t fit us in. We left and had an excellent meal at the Buxton Brewery. Moral of the story is book a dinner slot the day before.   There are no bathroom floor mats, you have to use a hand towel.  Rooms aren’t serviced, so if you want toilet rolls, tea bags, clean towels you have to collect these from reception.

The bacon was very hard and the eggs were cold Drafts windows

The location was amazing - a short walk into the centre of the town.   The building is impressive and ornate- lots of beautiful features to admire. Room was average size with basic furniture and accessories.   Cost of parking was reasonable. Continental breakfast was good and reasonably priced. We ordered a pizza from the bar menu on the Friday evening; it was possibly the worst pizza I have ever had in my life. I was unable to eat most of it.   Service - there were very few staff and those who were working, seemed to be disinterested in customers and were not welcoming or sociable. (Although, a couple of staff were incredible and seemed to be doing everything - bar, restaurant, drinks, food.)  Breakfast - the hotel was unable to cater for the number of guests, there was no turnover of covers. Guests were clearing tables themselves, getting cutlery out of the staff drawers themselves.  There seemed to be no staff actually clearing the tables to make room for subsequent guests.  It was a chaotic dining room.    There was an enormous convention on during the weekend we visited, which took over every suite in the ohtel and made it very crowded, noisy and we felt quite pushed out and as if we were not supposed to be there as we were not part of the convention - I felt that information about events on this significant scale taking pace should be shared with other guests as it is definitely not what I expected.   We had run out of toilet roll and no staff seemed interested in finding a replacement. My husband had to take one from the toilets in the leisure facilities as we had no choice.   Costs - we of course had to pay for our room, but then meals on top of that (we ended up eating elsewhere after the pizza on Friday), we then had to pay for parking, and then had to pay to use a chilly swimming pool.   Room - average size / decor, etc, but unpleasantly hot even with the window open.
Everything  The suite, the staff, the location, the food N/a

When we tried to book a table and found out we were then told we couldn't upgrade despite the rate, still being available online.

The room was adequate, but the sea view was amazing. We were able to lie in bed and watch the sea. situated beautifully in Buxton, helpful and accommodating kitchen and restaurant staff especially cath and her grandson who were extremely helpful and the restaurant manager also. the young ladies in the evening were happy smiley and accommodating. Anna in the spa who performed our treatments was really good at providing a beautiful experience. 10 minutes after we arrived we realised there wasn’t a fridge in our room I spoke the young on the desk who was very busy booking people in he called his manager who obviously wasn’t happy to come up from her desk, she did eventually come to reception and wasn’t very happy with me requesting to speak with her. I explained about my medical needs and as I had previously stayed in the hotel and had previously been provided with a fridge for my diabetic medication she said we couldn’t have one as they don’t have them in the room provided I asked if it was possible to have one in the room she said no. she suggested I used the fridge downstairs which would mean having to get dressed and go to take my insulin at 4 .30 am.  the first 24 hrs was very disappointing until we spoke to cath who understood the importance of  having my medication at the right temp she spoke  to her manager they called maintenance who by the time we had finished breakfast had a fridge in our room. The manager needs some retraining on customer care and stepping outside of the box to provide a service and make the guests life relaxing and an enjoyable experience. I can only imagine what her staff have to deal with. I have always enjoyed my stays at many of Britannia hotels but she put a damper on this occasion. apart from this managers unhelpful nature you really do have staff to be proud of and due to their helpfulness and accommodating service I will be returning. Food was also a plus beautiful.
Very nice hotel rooms were clean and the breakfast was lovely .good value for money. nan
Access to pool and gym facilities at no extra cost. Lovely food and restaurant No hot water on the first night, understand that this was unforeseeable and compensated for this.
Cleanliness and friendly professional staff comfortable rooms Nothing
Good breakfast   Good location Bigger choice of menu  Cheaper parking  More entertainment
Location,  breakfast, gardens No view from window and where dirty. But this room is a modified store room. But was clean and for 1 night was ok

Nice and handy to town shops and cafes. Things were left in bedside drawers obviously from previous guests. So checks need to be done. Also no parking provided so had to pay a fortune for public locked car park around the corner or risk parking way down the road where wasn’t safe during the night.

The room and bed were comfortable.  The shower was good.  The breakfast had a decent selection to choose from. The first person I met at the front desk was very unfriendly.  I got there a bit early ahead of check in time, which i knew...he told me to come back between 3-4pm. I said to him I thought the check in was at 3pm. He just answered and said you can leave your luggage and just repeated the time to me. I got back to the hotel at about 2:30ish....and another gentleman was there and he checked me in. He was much more polite and friendly.   I went to use the pool in the morning, around 09:45. They were scrubbing and cleaning the surface around the pool then. They also used a bucket and dipped it into the pool to clean the surface with that water. I found it inappropriate to clean around the pool whilst it was being used. I wasn't the only one there... two more people were at the time....plus it was unhygienic to be putting the bucket youre cleaning with into the pool for the water and then cleaning the surface whilst the hotel guests are using the pool.

Easy check in/check out. Left luggage on site. Right on the seafront. 2 mins walk to Lanes/restaurants/Pier.   Large family room for 4 on 4th floor so a lovely view.  Comfy beds.  Lovely pillows/duvet Warm room/radiator/could open window 10cm for fresh air though.    Swimming pool was lovely. Towels given too. Kids loved it. Great that it was open until 9pm.   Lovely breakfast. Fresh pastries. Cooked breakfast. Good coffee. Good teas. Sat by the window looking out over the sea. Would have been wonderful if both beds had been super king or king.  The double was a little small for the two kids to share.  Needed a bit of a refresh in painting and bathroom. BUT for the money and location it was great.
Spacious room. Comfy bed. Iron and ironing board. Bathrobes and fluffy towels. Close to Opera House. Couldn’t operate the coffee machine and there were no instructions
Room was spacious . Bed was comfortable. Swimming pool was as a little cooler than we would have liked. Steam room very tired. Sauna Broken.

Great atmosphere. Well modernised character hotel, lobby looked the part. Room was a little narrow, but clean and nicely decorated. Bar lacked seating! Just the bar was small, with inadequate seating, however, it looked plush and would have had a drink if we could have found a seat.

when we booked the hotel the first time they were asking £308 for a compact room which would off  been ok for us, but after checking back on booking.com found a standard room for £220 so cancelled the room at £308 and booked the £220 room which turned out to be a family room three beds for two of us. Location is very good a short walk from the train station, breakfast was very good great selection of food ate out for our other meals. BIG negative was that they wanted paying for the wifi in the room which was free down stairs , these days people want wifi in the rooms . though we never did but asking for payment for WIFI in the room
I've stayed many times. Cosy room 252. Quiet. Shame the pool wasn't open. Breakfast nice. nan

The staff are very helpful and our room was amazing the view from our room was beyond words. Lots of space in our room and very comfortable. And as a added bonus we had free use of the pool sauna and gym. Nothing we had the best stay I have ever experienced in a hotel

all good room and bathroom. having to have all restaurant seperately paid and not on room number was bit annoying.  hard to eat there as a guest unless had it booked ....a bit beforehand.    could do with some vetilation or airconditioning ,very hot weather....

Reception staff could not have been more friendly and helpful, in particular Dominique.The breakfast was by far the best we have had in a long time, even in more expensive hotels.Room very big and quiet(410) with great sea view. Honestly cannot find anything bad to say about our stay at this hotel.
The bed was very comfortable. The shower over the bath was lovely and powerful and hot. I think our room overlooked the boiler room and there was a hum from the boiler all night long.

Location was brilliant. Breakfast was very good, well stocked buffet and kept hot. Hotel being refurbished and reception, restaurant and conservatory very nice. Standard room outlook was poor with scaffolding and laundry area. Will pick a better room in future. Limited lunch menu and after 10 minutes from ordering told no burgers had been delivered so had to change selection. At least provided with a drink in compensation.

I do feel that making a last minute booking with booking .com puts you in one of their less desirable rooms !! Although the room was spotless it was right at the back of the property and involved lots of corridors and stairs to get to !! Love the building itself . Staff very friendly
Staff were efficient, food was good, comfortable bed. The room was serviced every day. The bedroom furniture was dark and outdated and would benefit from being modernised.

The room we stayed in was super spacious and the view was fabulous. The location was also convenient because it was right beside the promenade and also a tram stop The wait staff at breakfast were quite lax and the food and cleanliness in the bar was minimal

Right on the Beach Front, just opposite the Pier....lovely friendly staff......we were also kindly offered a free upgrade to (room 103) which had the best views ever as it was a family suite on the side of the building and the Stunning views of Beautiful Brighton Bursting on a Hot Summers day was spectacular!  Big rooms, clean, comfortable beds & white fluffy towels, I shared this suite with my bestie, 2 older children & also took along my 2 fur babies (the hotel is very dog friendly!)... we also made full use of the indoor swimming pool, sauna & steam rooms.... highly recommended for a little escape/break away! No hotel parking & it can get quite expensive parking around that area, so just be prepared and check car parking prices and include them in your budget before you  stay!
The room was clean and comfortable. The bar meals and drinks were reasonable. There was nothing to say we did not like.
Room was small and very basic, the shower took half an hour to get warm The room size

big room, good breakfast, great location, nice people from de bar/restaurant check in at 3pm but delay for 4pm. and I was always asking if the trim was ready. they told they would let us know, but after 30 more minutes I asked again and it was ready but no one talked to us in the sofa just in from of the reception! I have a kid and I'm also pregnant! the people from reception were always facing like "I don't wanna be here". not kind. seems to be mad. definitely shouldn't be working in a RECEPTION!  wifi not good in any area of the hotel.  but the worse is the reception team!

Position of Hotel was great very central  ,we had a sea view , we  could of looked out of the window all day! Breakfasts where great really good choice. Staff friendly and helpful.  We ate in the restaurant first night lovely food really attentive and polite staff. Decor Is a little tired bathroom could of done with updating but all functional.

The hotel interiors were lovely and location very good, apartment was really good size and had 3 TVs so a bit overwhelming in a good way Rooms could be redecorated and the swimming pool could do with refurbishment
fab central location could be a bit noisy, but that’s what you get for being so central!

my room was sea facing with a great view of pier. sunshine in room all morning. great bed. The room was recently decorated,clean and warm.Breakfast was good. but cereal and toast serving corner of dining room small and cramped. and to nicpic  cutting up the bacon hard to do because of the poor knifes. the sauna and steam room very good the lift doors close too quickly. someone will be hurt!!

We stayed in one of the family rooms and I was pleasantly surprised when we arrived. The room was very large, there was a king size bed and 2 single beds - not the uncomfortable sofa beds that you usually get in family rooms. Everything was very clean and well maintained. The location of the hotel was excellent. Overall it was a great stay The check out was very early, at 10am. Also although the bathroom was large, the toilet was so close to the bath there was minimal leg room.

They upgrade our room with no extra cost. We didn't actually need it but it was nice to have extra space.  The breakfast was a dream in terms of offer and presentation and all tables were clean.  The bed was very comfortable with a lot of extra pillows available. Netflix not working. Why have it on the tv if then is not possible to log in?  The pool is a bit small and there is no jacuzzi.  The bathroom, compared to the size of our room, was really small.  Radiators were off. The room was hot but having radiators off didn't help to drying my swimsuit after swimming so the day after I brought it home wet in a plastic bag.
Spacious, traditional, stylish, high ceilings, big hot bath, big bed. Dated furnishings.  Pool out of action.  Car vandalised on street outside. £9 for car park.

Very grand hotel, very relaxed stay and I enjoyed the pool and sauna!  9/10 for me if you take the hotel as it is and don’t compare with modern boring hotels Nothing could have been better for me!  Upgrade the changing room facilities in gym if I’m being picky.

Hotel check in brilliant , friendly helpful staff. Well stocked bar and freshly made cocktails . Food excellent . Bedroom spotless and ver6 comfy bed. Great facilities in room. Breakfast was excellent, fantastic value for money and great location. nan
breakfast 1 class room 1 class but bed was very hard for me the bed was to hard as i just had a hip done
Staff were lovely, room was bright and larger than I expected as well. nan
Friendly staff clean and good location The addition parking charges
Location - and seaview Not 'didn't like' but it is ageing and needs re-styling and general 'tlc' - but is it worth more to demolish and rebuild (as R/Albion next door)??!
Very good location by the sea and outside a tram stop Helpful staff Lots of options for breakfast Bathroom was a bit dated and the TV signal did not function

Location is amazing, food was good, rooms are huge and clean but very hot!! Very quirky hotel. Especially loved the pianist in the lobby Pool was very cold. The rooms were very hot, quite uncomfortably so at times.

Beautiful old iconic hotel overlooking sea and very close to Tower, room was comfortable and breakfast and staff were amazing, also nice Bar area to relax in. Looking tired in some areas but we were expecting that given the age of the building

The hotel does look very imposing and quite grand when you approach, there is a lift. The bed was comfortable On entering reception a wall of heat meets you, the whole reception area is dull and dreary it doesn't look like it has any kind of decoration since it was built. The room wasn't ready even though we arrived at 3.30pm and rooms are supposed to be guaranteed ready from 3pm. I asked if there was any A/C in the room, told of course not as it is a listed building. When we eventually got our keys, the room  was quite the maze to find. On entry the room smelled so strongly of bleach it took your breath away, the windows were stained with bird faeces, a light in the bathroom wasn't working, and the room was stifling hot.  There was a towel rail which was dangerously hot, which could not be switched off, this was right next to the toilet, and did not help the heat in the room. The shower would not stay at one temperature again we were told this is just the nature of an old building. So I guess you have to play the hot cold roulette each morning. The towels were old and rough  It's just not what I expect from a hotel of this supposed calibre

Spacious comfortable room. Hotel style full English breakfast- excellent. Centre of Buxton suited our needs perfectly. Good parking facilities, at an extra charge. Public areas were welcoming, warm and comfortable. Room radiator did not work so room was quite cold. Hotel staff provided a portable heater promptly.  No instructions as to how to work the television or the coffee making machine. The television in particular was very irritating.
The staff was super friendly, the location was stop on,the steam and sauna was alright. The place was clean and spacious nan

The location, and the architecture. Bedroom a good size. The mould on the shower curtain hem - a big no no for me. No safety grip rail near the  over the bath shower. Given the median age of the clientele, this was regrettable.

Breakfast was lovely and the staff helpful.  Dinner at the bistro was a little strange as pasta did not have a sauce as described in the menu.     Staff at reception were very helpful and informative. Unfortunately not an ideal venue for guests with walking difficulties.  The dining area has steps and the staff encourage guests to enter from an upper level.  we asked to enter from the lower level and this was ok.  Also to use the shower meant climbing into the bath this is not possible with walking difficulties.  We were offered a step but this didn't really make any difference.
Very friendly and helpful staff great breakfast clean rooms ..this was my 3rd stay in a year👍 No sky sports football
Good breakfast. Comfortable bed. Great room. Everything perfectly clean and well maintained. Friendly, helpful staff. nan

Location is great as it is opposite Brighton Pier. Breakfast was comical as they were short staffed that day and everything was running out faster than they could cook it. Had a room looking out to sea. The pool and gym facilities were very good and long opening hours too. nan
It was clean and welcoming and very good value for money. nan
Good location. Clean. Reasonable. All good. No complaints.

Good location with several transport options for getting around Blackpool. Excellent value fur money Decor in room a little tired and maid service poor. Dirty underwear found in a drawer but reception staff dealt with and very apologetic. Coffee/tea spills down walls not cleaned and pieces of paper on floor, lacked attention to detail

Location is fantastic for the beach and pier,  couldn’t fault the hotel or the breakfast choice and amount. The facilities were also excellent with a spa on the lower floor. We had a sea view double and there was ample room. The only thing I could find fault with was the temperature of the cooked breakfast. It wouldn’t put me off going back but it could have been hotter as we did get there early.

The breakfast was lovely but the staff didn't tell us how to carry on. The room was freshly decorated with new carpets.  The Palace is very convenient for the Opera House, just a short walk away. We didn't want to pay for wifi in the room. We discovered in the morning that the television in the room wasn't working. Reception staff were efficient but not friendly, could do with some customer training.  The pillows were too 'fat' for comfort and there was no choice.
location, staff, spacious rooms, breakfast and got an upgrade to sea view. nothing

Hotel has great character. Downstairs has wonderful rooms, reception area and bar is excellent. Room on Level 4. Window didn't open. TV only had 4 channels. Bed like a park bench. Looks like it waa last decorated just after 2nd World War. This was a very poor room.

Place and staff were super. It was clean and tidy. Would suit family or couples. Very well mentained couldnt fault it :) The food wasnt great to be honest. The breakfast wasnt nice at all but we ate locally at cafes and blackpool diners :)
Lovely historic building The room & bathroom were very dated

loved the gym, and pool. very clean, large comfy room. I found the floors to be extremely creaky, meaning that every time someone walked in the room above me, it made such loud noise that it hindered my getting off to sleep. but the bed itself was very comfy.
It was very clean and great location No plug sockets by bed
Nice size room …good breakfast…really just needs a little tarting up …but I guess after Covid not so much flying around..beautiful location.. nan

The room we had was a lovely big room. The bed was huge a very comfortable. The breakfast was tasty, with a lot of choice. There was nothing to dislike. If I was going to be really fussy, I maybe say the windows could do with some tender loving care. They were a little tatty looking.
fantastic staff from reception, cleaners, breakfast. 1st class and a fabulous room. Thank you 😊 nothing

Breakfast was ok, but not much choice for vegetarians. The staff were accommodating and friendly.  The beds were comfortable and the linen was spotless.  The location is in a fantastic, commanding position for the town centre. It’s literally across the road from the train station too. The room decor was very tired and the bathroom was dirty with age. The fan didn’t work.  The credit card style door key failed to work twice in two days, which wasn’t great when you’re staying on the third floor!   The pool also needs some maintenance as it has multiple mosaic tiles missing.

I stayed in a 'compact double room', it was clean and comfortable. Friends stayed as a couple in a similar room and said the same. The bed was very comfortable.  The stay was good value. I would definitely stay again and recommend. Pianist playing in the reception wasn't my taste, a bit outdated

Lovely hotel overlooking the sea. Friendly staff. Fascinating history to the hotel and the many celebrities, ex prime ministers and royalty who have stayed at the Imperial Hotel.  The breakfast was really good and served in the elegant Palm Court Restaurant.  Only stayed for one night and booked a standard room. It was clean and comfortable. Unfortunately barely got any sleep due to the very noisy extractors in the building right in front of our window. Shut the window but still the noise came through until approx 5 in the morning!  Room 142 not good if your expecting a nice view and a good nights sleep!
Excellent breakfast. Good location. For the price paid I would have expected the room to be better. It was a little tired and dated and needed a little TLC.

Arrived early, left our luggage very pleased to receive a call from the hotel that our rooms were ready by 2.00pm. Staff were very friendly especially in the bar in the evening. Good breakfast. Location very central.  Loved the use of the swimming pool, sauna and steam room. Windows were filthy, view would have been better through clean windows, and they were quite drafty. Lovely old building, outside in needs of some TLC.

The staff were all pleasant and helpful. The room was ready for early check-in. The room was spacious and clean, a couple of cupboards had seen better days but were sturdy enough. The beds were comfortable and the temperature warm enough but not too hot as is sometimes the case in hotels. The bathroom was sparkling clean and everything worked, powerful shower.     The hotel is very grand and has hosted many famous faces over the years. On the ground floor there is beautiful paneling and display cases. Nice seating areas around reception. Good variety of choice at breakfast, all well presented.    The Imperial is at the north end of the promenade, not too far out but means it is quiet. We were able to park at the hotel and the cost was £9 per night as expected. (It would probably be busier in the summer for parking) The wardrobe was fine but the cupboards in the main part of the room were rather worn. They would have been ok for storage if required but we didn't need to use them as only there one night. They provided a surface for the kettle and lamp etc.
Good room,comfortable bed, quiet and really close to airport parking. nan
Very last minute but excellent service, very clean and tidy and facilities were very good. nan

We've stayed here before and the hotel is obviously very convenient for Heathrow. The staff are friendly and helpful. Breakfast options are decent. The rooms are clean and comfortable. The price was very competitive too. Not much really. The spec of the hotel is a little old fashioned. However, we had no issues.
perfect for my early morning departure. best hotel of my 3 weeks travel. luxurious comfortable quiet nan

breakfast had a good variation of food and beverage. The hotel gave good access to the airport. Staff was overall friendly and open to every question. The room and bathroom was very clean, which we highly appreciated. The room was clean but gave from the design a prison feeling. The decor/ wall paint was not very welcoming and gave more a sad atmosphere. It was not much room for the suitcases and moving around in the room.
Location very handy for OS flight next day, view from room and Cloud Bar was excellent Wasn’t great value, check in did not offer binoculars and other items for upgrade stay
Location to international airport Doors slamming during the night
Close to airport and the staff DON'T USE THE HOTEL TAXIS! BOOK YOUR OWN. BLOODY RIP OFF £20 FOR 1.2 MILES Even a black cab is better value

Conveniently located next to T1 arrivals hall and coach departures; a huge plus after a long flight. Comfortable room and layout for stopovers. Good shower and supply of toiletries. Don't check out online until you are ready to leave, or the system will automatically shut the TV and lock you out.

Location great as we were travelling overseas.  Room fit for purpose (ie short stay).  Beds very comfortable. Self serve checkin/out. Cost of inhouse dining a little expensive.  Insufficient number of staff at reception to quickly manage queries.
Good bed, large shower, close to departures for an early stay. Breakfast in the airport.  Easy. Nothing, it fitted the purpose well.
no aircraft noises , good isolation difficult and small parking
The room was very clean and comfortable. Great bed and good shower. The food is awful. Had a chicken parmigiana and it tasted like something from Aldi’s freezer section.
Cleanliness; pleasurable breakfast/dinner; proximity to airport nan
Location, comfortable room. The shower screen was rather short and a fair amount of water went on the floor. Bathroom lighting not very bright.
super quick check in. They let us take our luggage cart into the room. shower and bathroom were great. bed was super comfy. nan

Fantastic location when needing an early international flight. Appreciated the wake up call as well. Staff efficient and friendly at check in and check out. Room clean and comfortable. Room service was good with reasonable selection Nothing
Location is good.  Dogs allowed.  Room was comfortable. nan

Room spacious ,luxurious and soundproofed with a heavenly mattress. Liked restaurant in the hotel as Sydney  international airport scant in that regard. Being mere steps from the International Hall was fabulous. Grouchy 5am breakfast clerk who was peeved when I politely asked where to dispose of my mini cereal packet.Being greeted by a smiling face in the morning is important.
Lovely big room, comfortable bed, nice hot shower Nothing
Very modern and clean.  Check in was seamless.  within walking distance to Sydney International Terminal. Limited pub menu

Perfect for an early international flight. Just to walk across to the airport was so good. No worries re taxi turning up or traffic problems. The hotel was comfortable, restaurant good and position ideal All good
Comfortable bed and pillows. Buffet breakfast was excellent with a wide range to choose from. Staff were friendly. Check in process could be improved by having more staff available.
Location & bathroom set up Nothing

The hotel was very convenient for Heathrow airport and the bus stop for the transfer to the airport was just outside the hotel.  The hotel was very good value considering its location. There was nothing I didn't like and I'd stay there again.
Airport location and ease of getting to Check in at the Airport Terminal. A higher rating could be achieved had the main Restaurant been open
Clean and comfortable bed. Staff very helpful and polite. There was nothing that we didn't like.
Comfortable beds  clean and quite rooms. Close to Heathrow Airport. nan
proximity to international airport standard and cost of food and drink

Comfy bed, good bathroom, short walk to food places nearby and good buffet breakfast. Unfortunately the curtain broke when we tried to pull it across but the staff came and tried to help us fix it. Airport noise wasn't too bad considering it's a stone's-throw from the runways. Rooms are a little cramped but as a stopover before a flight they do the job.
Staff & Bar/Eating Facilities Cash Payment not allowed  Hotel looking a bit old and tired
not far from the airport nan

Cheapest hotel of those close to the airport, on the shuttle bus route. Restaurant reasonable price for basic fayre The bathroom had a permanent waft of sewage about it.   The shuttle bus didn't turn up in the morning- it's a private enterprise so the hotel don't have any control over it. Staff said this is not unusual. There was a couple there who may well have missed their flights due to this so unless you have plenty of flexibility I wouldn't rely on it.
Amazing view and beds are so compfy I couldn't stay longer
The bed was so… comfortable. Convenience to the international airport for a 6am flight was so good. Online check in marvellous. Staff gave good advice about check in time. No complaints
Breakfast was excellent great choice, staff room restaurant, everything perfect highly recommend it value for money Nothing
The fact it was a short stroll from station to front door, then short walk from hotel to check in, allowing me to have a great breakfast before flight, without any hassles, can’t beat it! nan
the receptionist was lovely! room was clean nothing! enjoyed my night
Clean, comfortable, well located for Heathrow. Hoppa bus stops there hourly. Very quiet. Pleasant view out window. Restaurant is convenient. Carpet in room a bit shabby but no other issues
Close to airport. Very good dinner..M.mMy The safety door outside the bathroom.  Husbands walkre kept getting getting stuck.
The room was large and very well appointed. The hotel is close to the airport. The food and service in the restaurant and bar was excellent. Nothing. The staff were very helpful.

The breakfast was really good! It is so nice to start your day or onward journey with such a brilliant breakfast. The hotel couldn’t have been more convenient for us, being so close to the international terminal. I can’t think of anything that I didn’t like about the hotel (perhaps the choice of food in the bar/restaurant could be increased) but I would say that getting from domestic to international terminals was not a great experience. Nothing to do with the hotel but maybe your voice would help?
quiet room with comfortable bed croissants spelled incorrectly as “croissant’s” on the bill. A common modern error but looks unprofessional
Location to the airport and food A bit pricey
The location and the comfy bed.  Very big bed for  sleep with a baby and husband.  Nice shower.  The receptionist Yuli, was very nice helping us to pick our  room🥳 nan

Always love staying here when we have an early morning/late arriving flight into Sydney as we live 2 hours north of airport. Our 'go to' hotel as rooms are quiet and just across road from international airport. Also easy online check in and out. Nothing - always a great stay

Location was good as you are just across from international airport or you go across the road to catch a shuttle bus to domestic (if Qantas). There could have been a biscuit to have with coffee and for the price of the room real milk or at least a couple more of the little milks .
Very friendly staff. Lovely comfortable room. It's sort of in the middle of nowhere, needed taxi to get anywhere or buses.
The food was good. Staff very friendly and helpful. Comfortable beds. Clean rooms  Check out at midday. The room was a bit too hot.
Food and decor. Bathroom good. Noisy guests shouting in the corridors at night! Too hot in the rooms.

Close to airport for early morning flight . Clean comfortable . Restaurant opened early as well so able to take advantage of included breakfast. Wish we got in early they have a rooftop for drinks . Definately hotel of choice by the airport. Liked everything
Location to airport  Comfortable room No exhaust fan in toilet  Pillows very uncomfortable

Friendly and welcoming reception staff. Basic but clean room with good bathroom. Good bar, reasonably priced restaurant. Good overnight parking. Close to airport and on Hoppa route. No noise at all - the triple glazing really does work! nan
Location great and breakfast amazing. Couldn’t get room service at 11pm at night. Rang numerous times but no answer.
The room was clean,  good hot shower, good basic breakfast. Ideal for one night stay. nan
Typical Ibis room, has all you need for a one night stay. Clean and comfortable. Staff could be friendlier.
Room, nearness to airport and even the breakfast was excellent nan
Great location for International flights.   Nothing else comes close.   Friendly professional staff.   Great facilities.  You get what you pay for. nan
Close to airport nan

I was very happy with the Ibis hotel, my room was small but clean, had everything I needed for an overnight stay prior to seeing my son off on his flight the following morning from Heathrow.  Price was good value for money.  I did not eat at the hotel so cannot comment on the food available.  Staff were friendly and helpful.  Staff booked me a taxi to take me to Terminal 4 in the morning - for reference £30 I don't know what the hopper would cost or other public transport - it was worth it for me as I had my sons luggage with me to transport as  he had spent the night in London.  I would be happy to stay again and happy to recommend. Nothing to add here.

I was able to drop my bags at left luggage at 10.30am including having some items that needed to be kept cool put into fridge storage. Then I was free to go off on a beautiful coastal walk on Xmas Day. The room itself was great. The glazing was incredible keeping out all external noise whilst giving a view of the airport/runways and I enjoyed watching planes land and some of the smaller ones take off (the big planes needed more runway beyond my vision). Delicious half bottle of full bodied red wine in the minibar with my little picnic whilst watching planes and chatting to best friend in UK. Great shower Ideally placed for early flight the next morning even if price is a bit above average Nothing

I loved the location and my room was perfect - clean, spacious and super comfortable Dining areas were noisy and relatively crowded and expensive!a simple coffee and pastries would have been very welcomed!

Staff (so kind, so helpful, so sweet), room, breakfast, dinner, free parking...Everything was perfect for me and my dog for a 1-2 day rest before we crossed to France. Drinks at the bar are more expensive than the posh areas in London.

Location is OK, but would probably prefer something more central, particularly if wanting to shop.   This was a nostalgic selection after staying there many years ago with my late brother.  Deluxe room was spacious and well appointed. Even had a large bathtub, which was most appreciated.  We did not eat in the hotel as preferred to experience the local flavours in food courts. It took 3 requests, in person, with the Reservation Desk to discover that we COULD actually reserve and pay $178 for a Late Checkout from our room. Trying to make alternate arrangements had caused a reasonable amount of stress up till then.
Clean room and convenient location. nan

My stay was during Christmas eve, received cookies at the reception for my son, the lift was never congested, the room amenities with great bath tub, the Christmas decoration was superb, we were greeted with mascot on Christmas eve morning, the ambience and holiday experience was just amazing! No bidet. Wish there is.

Great sea view from our room after we upgraded! And great location on the harbour, and for driving to Dover or Canterbury etc! Very dated. Room smelt a bit strange. Also I would say this is a hotel for older clients… I think we bought the average age down and felt a bit like we were in the tv show Benidorm at points.

Great location , good price A little tired and in need of an update?  Staff looked overworked ( checkin 4:00pm, room not ready until 5:30pm - felt so sorry for the cleaners)  Reception again looked stressed and not entirely happy ( probably again due to the queues of people waiting to check in)
- Good Central Location - Great Views - Efficient Staff - Rooms and Bathroom felt rather dated - Quite expensive for what you get

Breakfast buffet excellent as is the hotel's location - easy walkability to many attractions. I fail to see how this hotel could charge the full price (I was lucky to get an exceptional 50% off) but I could not see the full price value - hotel room was great but nothing outstanding. However I do appreciate they have a LOT of staff, very friendly and helpful which I guess is also possibly management's biggest expense to cover and all the staff I interacted with also very knowledgeable and approachable.
Room clean and comfortable. Staff friendly and welcoming. nan
Central location, clean and good service. Nil

Nestled in an exceptional location, the hotel offers close proximity to all amenities, shops, and attractions, making it a highly convenient choice. Many attractions are within easy walking distance, and public transport is conveniently accessible nearby. The room provided a comfortable and relaxing environment, and the excellent WiFi service ensured seamless connectivity throughout our stay. Furthermore, the breakfast buffet surpassed our expectations, offering a delightful array of delicious options. Overall, our experience at the hotel was truly excellent in every aspect. nan
Very comfortable bed, excellent location. We were not able to upgrade which we asked to do as it was my birthday.

The location was great too, short walk from bars and tram stops. The air-conditioning vents on the balcony let out a lot of hot air so every time we went out on the balcony we got very hot. It was very difficult to find the right shower temperature - was either scolding or freezing!

I stay here everytime I'm in Melbourne and it's always excellent value for money, The staff are nice ,the food is good and so is the rooftop top pool I would have rated it excellent but not this time with the elevators playing up,or not working at all at times made it a big effort to come and go during the stay

Breakfast was excellent Booked superior but given Disabled room, all the hospital equipment very unpleasant especially for such a high price. Told staff and she said very busy so too bad!

The location is incredible! Sat out having drinks in the evening sun looking at the castle at the bottom of the street was just beautiful. Quick walk down to the cricket pitch and castle walls, access to the beach too. The restaurant is exceptional! The best fish and chips I have ever tasted! Gorgeous styling throughout the ground floor area, reception, bar area and restaurant were gorgeous. Room was comfortable, absolutely huge bed and tv with all channels. Room had all amenities, iron, hairdryer, lovely fluffy clean towels and crisp clean bedding.  Plenty of sockets around the room too. The only thing I could fault was that the carpet and chairs in our room had very obvious deep stains. Cream carpets are not ideal but overall we were very happy.

On arrival the staff were exceptional . It took us one minute from the station.. We had a high tea which only cost us £11 . The staff arranged our taxi to the football and back. Good location very convenient for dining and get to attractions such as theatre, ACMI.  Reception staff were very helpful. nan
The location was amazing The room was large and the bed was comfortable The shower was excellent The carpet could have been cleaner Some of the waitresses needed more training
Room, location, comfort, nothing
great location , very affordable prices nan

Location was very good and central to everything cosy hotel good prices on hotel bar impressed. Breakfast was good could have been warmer.
lobby is beautiful this hotel value decline stayed here couple times and this visit it has change a lot it looks dirty now broken down elevator just dirty

The room was good staff so nice,, ladys in the dining room for breakfast where great we will be back again this was our 3 trip and stay here at the station hotel thank you 💞🌺 Tony Chris Rice nan
location was great. menu and food were amazing too bedroom was quite small and an unusual shape but had good views

Location was amazing lical to everywhere, shops, bars, river you could walk to most tourist places within 15minutes. We had window open as it was hot and it was a bit noisy, but this was out of the hotels control and not really an issue for us we slept amazing.
