Nice atmosphere, quiet room,good facilities. Very clean. I liked everything.
Best location that u can have, great breakfast, price oldschool rooms
The rooms were very clean,the staff was nice,the pool was beautiful. We will definitely stay again nan
Very quiet location, room upgrade was well worth it for the view over the river. Friendly staff, excellent food, drinks and service. nan
Hotel location is great. The staff are extremely welcoming. The room was clean . The room was on the ground floor so we were kept awake most of the night by people outside.
Location, staff, dog friendly, food, atmosphere Room we had looked a bit tired, especially the carpet, but we know some rooms are being updated.

The hotel was spacious and airy. They upgraded us to a family room, which was fantastic and at the same price  So the bedroom was great  I did think that the bath/shower might benefit from a grab rail but that's an observation and not a criticism  The breakfast was really good - scramble egg and salmon on sourdough - and the coffee too  The staff were great coping well on a very busy weekend All was good

I had  a large high bed in the large room in a central location, from which I could walk to museums, art galleries and restaurants.  The breakfast buffet was substantial, with plenty of choices.   All facilities were good and the staff were very helpful. I can't think of anything!

The friendliness of staff; the subdued decor of the foyer and comfort of the room (the bathroom renovations are great); the central location and affordability. The noise level of the a/c system outside. The ‘early’ morning renovations taking place on the floor below were not pleasant to wake up to, so it would have been good to be forewarned.

Our room was clean and tidy, the beds were very comfortable and the bathroom, especially the shower, was top-notch. The breakfast was great, plenty of choice and the staff were helpful and friendly. The location was fabulous. I thought that the room was a bit "tired" and could probably do with updating.

location was great you can walk around the hotel there are many restaurants and stores some are a bit farther but no need to drive or Uber around if u don’t mind a 15 min walk was hoping to at least be able to have Netflix on the tv and my room from the minute I got in was wet all over the floor the ac was leaking constantly and progressed to the point where I had to wear Sandles in almost every part of the room or I would be in a puddle I contacted guest services to get it fixed they brought someone in and he said it was due to the rain I had told him that it wasn’t the rain because it happens all day. he cleaned the mess and just left and few hours later my room was soaking wet again. and our elevator was constantly being smoked in by staff using vape pens
We were made really welcome by all the staff not just those on reception. Room was spacious and location was fabulous. Car park discount was much appreciated too! Nothing

Good location and value for money Cafe staff.. never smiles like we came there no value for them. Food for breakfast not value for money because not really fresh or not much choice. Since I enter eat & out from your cafe.. your staff never smile even one’s.

Good location, free car park, affordable, clean bed Rooms are very tired in need of decoration. Some lights didn't work and lack of electric sockets. Room was also a little musty, possibly because of some damp.
Location, staff and food were all great. No A//C so traffic noise with windows open…. The pillows were poor quality.

In the shower the vent has a lot of accumulated  dirt. Also the shower  has a step to climb in and out of it with a  hand rail for support. We were very care not to fall when climbing in and out when the floor was wet. ( We are both over 80 years of age with back and knee problems) Other than that the location was very good. The staff were very helpful and considerate, The food was excellent . The room was very good .   Thank you we will be back if we were able to in the future . Excellent accommodation nan
Location of the hotel,  we booked a superior room with a river view and it was lovely  comfortable bed. Lovely breakfast. nan

The location on International Drive was very convenient for my schedule, and the view from my 9th floor room (down International Drive toward Icon Park) was very enjoyable. The room was also extremely spacious and the bed quite comfortable. Everything was clean and the staff at both check-in and the small café in the tower lobby were extremely friendly and helpful. Parking by the area near my room's location (the tower) was very crowded and there was very little soundproofing between rooms. Several aspects of the room itself, too, were in definite need of updating/repair (broken tiles in the bathroom, non-functioning lights, peeling/cracked paint, etc.) and the shower head was so low that it barely cleared the top of my head (I'm 5'7").

Easy 4 minute walk to tram stop.  Walking distance to lots of restaurants.  Good sized room and very comfortable bed.   Very well priced for a hotel so close to everything in the centre of the city.  There is even a Woolworths Metro just up the road. Not the hotels fault, but the roadworks outside on the footpath made wheeling luggage a bit of a chore.  The poor hotel staff had to continually try and keep the dust of the steps.
Fantastic location and 2 resturants on site. Comfy, big bed Old bathroom, old tv system, needs updating
Lovely big room . Warm . Room with a view over river ouse . No air con in room and it was warm bit stuffy.
the bedroom was a good size and warm. The bathroom clean and a good supply of hot water. in the bar the fire was not on.

Room spacious and nicely decorated, warm, and larger than normal television. We paid extra for breakfast which was lovely and good value for money.  The hotel is in  a prime position by the river and only a very short walk into the centre of York. We don't mind paying a fee for parking but thought £17 per day was a little excessive.

room was clean food was nice car park was a bit small.  the staff were very pleasant especially the bar man called charlie who tried his best at irish coffees and he had never made them before what a lovely man he was. more choice on the evening meal menu

Good location to local entertainment. Hotel rooms were older although I could tell they were renovated several years ago but rooms fell short of expectations even on a cheaper$$ end. Did not use pool which looked nice and a large room with bar and a pool table will be opening soon which would add a positive to the hotel.

Best place that we have stayed during our trip to Scotland. Hotel well situated next to the railway station. Room that we did get was spacious, very comfortable for staying with a baby. The location was very convenient. The staff are all so nice and welcoming. We only stayed for 2 nights but still have room service and I appreciated that. Please post the exact room that we will have to stay in next time! We paid good money for it and the room quality was unsatisfactory. The waiting time for check in also way too long. central to everything and very clean amazing breakfast staff happy to help nan
Location excellent, great access to ferries and close to restaurants. An old building which was a bit tired looking but this just reflects its era (but very comfortable nonetheless).
It’s location and affordability was great! nan

Nice Breakfast nan

A quite hotel near Queens way street , tube & bus stations The bathroom is too small

the property was just absolutely beautiful! it’s close to shops and the train station which was very helpful! nothing, it was absolutely beautiful.

Great location. Within Walking distance of Seaport and Old Town. Self check in features. Secur baggage storeroom. Very clean and good value for dollars No TV,  and room would only be cleaned for a fee

Huge renovations ongoing and no mention of this when booking, some rooms outdated and tiny, we paid £460 for our room and we initially were given a tiny double room with a very small shower, no A/C so no heating and our plug in heater smelt like burning so didn't risk using it but the room was freezing, water boiling hot and wouldn't ever be cold, even the sink water was hot to brush teeth with.
Location was great for Paddington station and Hyde park Room smelt musty. Shower wasn’t great, no extractor fan.

The carpet looked dirty, the hallways had a funny smell, carpet in the room had stains - it was cleaned I could tell, but just old. The room had no air conditioning just a small heater.

Typical hotel room but very clean and very modern. Amazing location and comfy beds. No mini-fridge I the room but very nice communal kitchen with paper essentials (spoons/forks/plates) two fridges and two microwaves. A bit on the pricier side. I was expecting bigger room  but that's a nitpick.

Easy to find location. Room spacious and clean No prior notice the lift was out of order. Had to access my room through a stairway, which in reality was a fire exit/storage space. 3 flights of stairs.

Great location - next to old town and within a walking distance of pretty much any place in the city centre. Everything worked as expected. Can do everything via self service but could also seek help from staff if needed. Avoid the “atrium rooms” if you can, feels a bit claustrophobic. The ones with regular windows are great though.

It's more of a luxury hostel rather than a hotel. I wasn't prepared for how sparse the room would be, but it was comfortable and big enough, and once I got used to it - and realised I had access to a fridge in the shared kitchen - I enjoyed it. I asked for a quiet room as I planned to work in it for several hours each day, and it generally was - though there were some construction works nearby that were noisy, and there were a couple of nights on which a DJ was playing loud music in the recreational space behind the hotel. I liked the fact that there was a thick curtain over the very large window that was effective in blocking out the light (of which there's a lot at the time of year I stayed there). Would happily stay here again. Not much. But I couldn't understanding the prioritising of amenities in the rooms. The principle seems to be that there is almost nothing, yet there was a hairdryer - something I never use. I'd rather have had a kettle, or at least a real glass instead of a plastic one, instead.

Hotel is new and modern.  Location is excellent: 8min walk to ferry terminal.  Room is clean.  Beds are OK. Shower room - excellent.  One window inthe room can be opened. The location was very good,  central to access the west end, the staff were very pleasant and helpful 👍 I would definitely recommend this hotel and would stay there again. The only thing was the room was compact but very comfy.

The general appearance of the hotel is really very good, the lobby, corridors and bar are all quite impressive.  We had drinks in the Gild Bar which was a very pleasant area to relax in. As a family of five we had two rooms on the same floor - one of these rooms was significantly better than the other.  I assume that one room has been more recently refurbished than the other.  Our family King room looked tired and a little shabby in places.  The bathroom was rather unappealing - poorly lit, a slight drainage odour and some of the bathroom tiles, sealant  and plugholes looked quite old and unpleasant.  I believe that the 'better' room was an upgrade which would of course account for the higher standard to a degree, however even so I don't feel that the general appearance of the family room reflects the price we paid for it.

This hotel is literally 300m from the airport, so it takes 5 min to get on foot to the airport entrance. My singe room was spacious enough, and location of hotel is unbeatable since there is very limited public transportation between downtown Reykjavik and the airport. In my opinion, this hotel is extremely overpriced. There is no competition to it and with no public transportation between downtown Reykjavik and the airport, people have no choice but to stay in this overpriced hotel if they are having an early departure from KEF (the airport is nearly 50 km away from Reykjavik). Comparing value for the money with what one gets, the price, in my opinion, should be lowered by at least 30-50%.   Furthermore, furniture is dated/scratched/chipped/pealing; breakfast very standard (for the night's price, choices should be extraordinary, in my view); there were only 5-6 channels on TV with no major international stations, so you need to have access to online movies since TV in hotel room has nothing of interest to offer.

The property is close to Southern Cross station.  We came in on the SkyBus after 11 p.m. so a five minute walk was fine.  The room was big with a sofa, desk and luggage area.  Bathroom was a reasonable size.  The bed was very comfortable, water was hot and it had tea and coffee facilities.  Check in was easy which is what you want when you have just had a long flight.  We didn’t have breakfast or use the parking but I did notice the parking was $25 per night.  The WiFi, for me, just did not connect at all.  Connected but no internet, however my partner was able to get on without a problem.  This was annoying as I did have things to check but was too tired to bother about it.  We actually slept in last check out and woke to reception ringing at 10.30 a.m.  grateful to be given another half hour to get up and out.  Check out easy too.  Would recommend for overnight stay and probably a longer stay too. nan

The room is small but well equipped, the bed was comfy and let's be honest, you don't need more! I knew I was gonna be out visiting the city all day so I booked a room with no window and it was perfect. The location can't be better. All the staff members are super nice and helpful. nan
A good airport hotel just a few mins walk to the main terminal building. Lovely staff that were always there to help and a nice comfy room with an airport view to admire 😎 Nothing !
Location, restaurant, quiet and dark ideal for sleeping Inability to change reservation, price higher than the HH rate

Convenient everything was close very nice room was nice and you would think there would be a lot of noise from the road but when in room it was quiet would definitely come again Eggs from breakfast was to runny and sausage had funny taste but gravy and biscuits was good but it came with room so definitely a plus saved a lot of money

location for the NEC exceptional  disabled facilities very good  breakfast fantastic  staff were helpful, friendly and went the extra mile for us the cost was a little high but understand that HOYS was on at the time
Everything. The breakfast was good, the pool...& the heart-shaped jacuzzi tub was awesome!! We were celebrating our one year anniversary & it was fabulous!! nan

Amazing hotel, just across from airport and monorail that takes you to the NEC centre. Amazing staff. Rooms were immaculate, food was lovely. Having the screen with arrivals/departures in the hotel lobby was so amazing. Couldn’t hear a thing surprisingly since the airport is so near!! Would definitely recommend!! nan
you won’t closer to the airport. breakfast was surprisingly good (quality and quantity), but my expectations were very low given the (almost monopolistic) location of the hotel nan
Perfect location, 5 min walk to airport. Breakfast from 4.30am, great choice for a morning flight Convenient but doesn’t worth 300 euro for such a basic room

The staff was super polite and helpful.  The location is amazing. Right in the center of London just a minute away from Trafalgar square. The premises were clean and the bed was comfortable. I know it's stated on the description, that's why I have put a very good score, but the room is super small. There is not even a cupboard, just hangers, hanging from the wall. There is no space to put a suitcase.  Also the toilet is built in the room with glass walls that don't secure any privacy at all.
Very modern clean room, small room but enough room for us for the 2 nights we stayed. Staff very friendly. Only down side bed a little hard.

Location great, very clean, great breakfast (included) Drove up with a number of suitcases and had to struggle to get them out, find a trolley and put them in the foyer. Then took the hire car to be dropped off. Would have bern nice to have had some assistance!

Rooms are clean, employees are kind and helpful, dinner was good. We did sleep well, wasn't noisy at all. A bit expensive, but the location (300 m to the airport) is perfect if you have to take an early flight. Breakfast starts at 04.30 (we didn't have). Rooms are a bit worn-down; almost the whole bath floor was wet after showering
How friendly the staff was..Also the room was clean and comfortable..Breakfast was nice nan
Great location, staff were fantastic, good car parking Beds were a little too soft for my liking.

The hotel is stunning and the service is exceptional, really lovely staff!   You get a glass of prosecco and a guided tour on arrival.   We stayed in the Windermere suite with an amazing view and a hot tub, which was really luxurious.   The breakfast is great and well cooked.  Try out the restaurant as this is fantastic.   The spa is the best I've ever been in, so lovely and the poolside bar is a real bonus!   The cocktails in the bar are of great quality! The bathroom in our room really let's it down for such an expensive suite, it's tiny!   You do feel a little overlooked in the room and in the hot tub  They should serve proper coffee at breakfast, the urn of coffee is not great! I asked for a cappuccino on the 2nd morning, which they did make but it's not made obvious that you can request this....  Finally paying £12 a night for parking is a bit of a joke when you've paid so much for the room!   Bit all this considered I would 100% return and recommend as it was just niggly things.
Convenient for airport. Early breakfast available. Comfortable beds. More choice at Aura

was very comfy and room was very clean there were no menus in room for either the Arbour Restaurant or the Bar area. Restaurant was extremely dark, so much so we could not see the menu. very expensive too.
Lovely spacious room, good breakfast, excellent  food in restaurant and superb spa facilities. Faultless

The price,  location .. hot water We had to call about the toilet being stopped up and we had to unstop it ourselves..Didn't pay to stay somewhere to have to do that ourselves when it was stopped up when we came back in the room that night.. Also, the bathroom sink needs more attention.. Toothpaste was all over the faucet and the bottom of the Mirror..  🤦‍♀️

The room was extremely comfortable! Nice size room and the bed was huge.  Spa Facilities were great, we made good use of the hot tub and cold pool.  The breakfast was lovely. Great variation of what was available. The cooked buffet tasted fantastic.  Location was good, a short walk away from the town where there were lots of bars and restaurants. Sauna was not very hot and the steam room was not very hot/steamy either.

We never had breakfast at the hotel but the location was excellent, in very close proximity to all the hussle and bussle of covent garden and trafalgar you could ask for Having a small window to open would have been nice but it certainly wont stop us coming back
Everything went well. Booking was as expected, great room on a high floor. Breakfast and dinner excellent. It was all good.  Nothing else to say
I have stayed several times at Altlantis and very good value for location. nothing really - I know what I am getting when I book.

Room clean and comfortable. We were going to a concert at the   world Resort Arena so proximity of hotel and venue was important. It was a pleasant 10 minute walk.  Did not stay for breakfast as had to be home early the next day.  Evening meal in the restaurant overlooking the lake was very good. nan

The property was in immaculate condition. The staff were super nice, helpful and very friendly. The room we stayed in was all round 100/10 I would fully recommend. The food was delicious from the breakfast the dinner and tea selection. Amazing experience Nothing.
Clean, new, large room, friendly staff Nothing.
Good location, central to London attractions. Room a bit small but expected for the price in central London.
All the staff are fantastic.  They run a very tight ship. Rooms are spotless, food is great. nan

Location 1st class for airport just a short 564yrds walk over the road,, sorry but flight was too early for breakfast thankyou so much for making my wife and sister in law stay excellent ❤️ There was nothing to not like
It was excellent value for moey, open 24/7, right near shops and an outlet mall, they have a heated swimming pool and vending machines.  Perfect. Everything was great -  I'll stay there again. 😄
size, breakfast dirty bed, dust in corners of desk, lots of little bugs, loud AC,

Location ecellent, close to Skybus, and tram, central to Park and close to Victoria Park, many eateries around but not too busy.  The room was beautiful, excellent view and very clean.  I like the swimming pool and gym too. nan

We landed late evening so this hotel was perfect for our first night in Iceland The hotel is within walking distance from the airport  and  handy also for collecting our hire car the next day. The bedroom and bathroom were spacious and clean. Breakfast is plentiful. For a airport hotel we were very impressed Nothing

Hot tub in the room was amazing, breakfast good and quality of the hotel was excellent. Some of the spa let it down a little, one of the hot tubs in there looked broken around the edges and the water in the pools seemed a little cool.
Easy check in, close to station and tram stops. Price was great nan

Room and hotel were exactly as pictured.  Check in was super easy, adequate parking and loved the outdoor & indoor pool, even if they are pretty small.  My daughter said that was her favorite part of the weekend.  I was grateful for the indoor pool, as it was very cool for late April.  The staff was able to accommodate our early check in time, and everything was clean and accessible.  It is a very busy area of town, but noise was not an issue and we enjoyed our stay.  There are some terrific restaurants within walking distance (Bullfish Grill and the Local Goat) and the Hollywood Wax Museum is right across the street.  Thank you for a great stay! The indoor pool is super small.
The staff were brilliant, the room was exceptional and the spa was first class. nan

room was spacious and beds.were comfy Asked for handicap accessible room. Asked for shower chair to which the staff said she doubted they would have one. My stepson asked if she could check and she did so begrudgingly.  She found one! No bar in shower. No balcony to walk out onto and   not on the river as described.
Location excellent for reason for visiting Didn't find anything to dislike

The bed was so comfortable and cosy. The room was exceptionally clean. Perfect location and friendly staff. The price. But expected as it's expensive all across London. The room was a shoebox and no where for luggage but also as expected and as advertised.
basic but very clean and comfortable nan

I like the rocking chairs on the river the most. The facility is in a great location.The staff is very friendly. The rooms are very clean, most with a balcony. We were in  308 and had a view of the water. I will be staying again. nan

Room was adequate although it was a corner room. Had hairdryer, safe, iron and ironing board.   Breakfast had a excellent selection. General nice hotel. The bed was too hard. Worse thing was the food menu in the bar. I appreciate that a convention was on, but all food was pizzas, burgers, etc. A chicken dish would of been nice. Even the dessert’s weren’t very appealing. Only ate there once because of selection.   I guess if you like they type of menu …. Happy days.
Great location.. really clean.. lovely staff.. nothing not to like. Perfect for the NEC. nan
We found the hotel very comfortable,  location was perfect.  Breakfast was great plenty of  choice. Nothing
Location.to train station and skybus great nan
Location near airport.reasnably clean They are adding a new section or something.was a crazy smell of chimical in all corridors and floors .

The location was perfect, hotel was gorgeous inside, decorated lovely & smelt lush!    To top off our stay Mark was fantastic and took us in the Bentley into town, where he showed us the best places to eat and how to get back 😊 Pillows were a little firm.
Great location - 5 mins walk to the lake but also quiet  Staff are very friendly and welcoming, cocktail pub is great and cosy nan
Location, and rooms were  clean and nice Free breakfast for the price would have been good.
we could walk to the departure terminal at Keflavik. very comfortable room. Good breakfast even at 4am. nan

The bed was very comfortable and breakfast was lovely. Our room was basic, and we couldn't have been further from reception.  The view was awful. If I'd booked on the day, I could have had it half price!  We had a few drinks in Resort World and was paying £11 in the sky bar, it was £19 in the Hilton.  Would I stay again...No!!!

We have stayed twice, and plan on staying here next time we are in the area. Everything was great. We enjoyed both the indoor and outdoor pools. Our granddaughter loved watching the ducks swimming in the river. Everything was great, no complaints.
The location was great. The staff was super friendly. The room were cleaned. The beds were not comfortable.
great location for airport as always great breakfast and comfortable rooms, friendly staff nothing all good

Good location for NEC. beds were comfy. Good tea, coffee making facilities. The rooms and bathrooms are very dated, as are the communal areas. The lighting in the bedroom was poor. Car parking spaces are too tight packed together.
Great location, room and staff. at a reasonable price. Have stayed here many times and will do so again I hope. nan

The spa was relaxing and the hotel was well decorated throughout. The staff were very helpful and always on hand to help. There were no charging stations for electric vehicles. There seemed to be lots of hidden fees. There was as additional payment for use of the car park unless you booked directly with the hotel. What I pay should cover that. Additionally there is a £10 fee for room service. Just include all these things in the price! No one wants to pay more than they have to. The rooms whilst nice were a badly designed. Our bathroom was very small and you had to squeeze between the door, shower screen and sink to get in and out of the shower. The shower was awful. No pressure and barely warm on the highest setting. Just disappointed as the reviews all said wonderful things and even the hotels self certified 5* rating gave us high hopes but overall it was a bit of a let down. Barely 4*. The restaurant was ok, felt awkward and cramped. The food was nice but not what we thought it would be after the high recommendation from the concierge.
nice staff and comfortable bed Right on the road
It was clean and the beds were comfortable.  Breakfast was ok The parking was a bit confusing and trying to figure out the best way to get in and out.

Beautiful hotel,helpful and friendly staff,we purchased Dinner and Breakfast at hotel which was lovely really enjoyed. Close to Resort world arena which we attended a concert, also close to shops,train station and airport. Would highly recommend. nan

the hotel is beautifully designed and feels very luxurious. The staff was friendly and helpful. The free upgrade was greatly appreciated. The spa facilities are wonderful and relaxing. It was an amazing stay. Nothing it was amazing. Thank you.

Everything about this hotel, and for the purposes intended (last night before flying out), was perfect: location, size of room, parking, breakfast starting at 4:30am, etc. The restaurant was too small for the number of guests staying at the hotel. A reservation system would help.

Dinner and breakfast were good, straightforward and plentiful.  Swimming pool very good  - better size than many hotels and great to see a decent kids pool. Sauna good temp.  Foyer unusually a space you would actually sit for a while. It seemed to work well for the folk listening to the live music. Reception could have been a lot more welcoming on first arrival. Somewhat dour!  Room very warm so slept poorly (with window open).
Rooms a little dated but it is an old property, good location to the centre of Southport. Excellent buffet style breakfast Room was a little cold and we requested a small heater which was granted
Price and location It was far too hot ! Tiny window. Top floor . We hardly slept. It was ridiculous
Staff amazing  Room fabulous  Onsite car parking  Food was great Complimentary tea coffee kitcats Variety of bar snacks limited
Free airport shuttle nan

Good parking facilities at an inexpensive rate.  Staff were all very pleasant.  They allowed us to check in early.  The room was clean and comfortable.  The hotel is dog friendly.  Close to all the amenities of Southport.  Right on the promenade. The room was very hot but then again so was the weather. Air con is needed.
Breakfast was good location was good nan
Good size room, wonderful shower.Ideal location for 02 academy. Nothing of note.
Comfy rooms N/A

Very convenient; appreciated the gesture of free tea/coffee at reception while renovating. The renovations did not impact my stay at all. really liked my room that faced a small grove of trees although I recommend the hotel arranges a litter pick-up in the small tree grove - would improve views

Down to earth staff, good location, clean room, comfy bed. Lovely bar man Simon.  The fact dogs were just roaming around with owners was great to see. Communal areas not very clean.  Water wasn't very hot Radiators didn't work No telephone in the room so had to physically go to reception if we needed anything.  Window didn't open very far.

Fantastic location right across the harbour! Perfect for exploring Hobart and very close to most tour departure points    The hotel has a lovely feel to it with lots of character and was very comfortable nan

Location, great parking free. Easy access to bath on foot. The building is very well kept and decorated. Very hot water. The spa although busy. Housekeeping /staff actually open external walkway/corridor doors to let fresh air in ! Amazing sooo many hotels smell of stale food and are toooo warm Bathrooms look a little dated but they are really clean.
Beautiful hotel with heated outdoor pool.   Walking distance to Bath, through historic section of city. Nothing

Breakfast was everything you could wish for good food freshly cooked,buffet is ideal for anyone wanting to eat and get on with their day,pleasant and helpful staff. Nothing obvious,found no faults service by staff was excellent.

Friendly staff. Smart room. I liked the motion sensor lights under the bed and the ability to cast Netflix from my phone to the TV in the bedroom. Good shower. Comfy bed. Free drink on arrival was a nice touch. The air conditioning unit was quite loud. The food wasn't very good.
Lovely rooms, lovely grounds, lovely staff, lovely lounge for pre-dinner drinks. The spa is very tired now and really needs a refurb to take it back up to original standards.
excellent location with breakfast included nan

Great location and really friendly and helpful staff. Bed was very comfy and our room was quiet. Breakfast was lovely, great choice and again, lovely staff. Would definitely stay again. The cleaning in the bathroom could do with some attention to detail (limescale build-up) and the rooms in general are a little dated and worn.
Good location in the old quarter near the waterfront and a good restaurant downstairs. nan

Funky modern style, well appointed. Loved the relaxed breakfast format. Excellent location for SEC Hydro. The bathroom shower gel and shampoo dispensers were hilariously frustrating. 100 pumps for a teaspoon despite full bottles. Such a tiny detail, but was annoying. Everything else was 1st class.

Excellent location, close to docklands & range of nearby eateries, Salamanca market within walking distance. Great inclusive & delicious breakfast options, spacious & clean room. Can get noisy with weekend night crowds. Limited TV channel options. A smart TV so you can login to your own streaming accounts would be awesome.

Great location and reasonably priced for a spa hotel in Bath. Also love that it’s dog friendly - the staff brought a bed and treats for my dog which was lovely. Outdoor area at the spa doesn’t have many loungers when it’s super sunny, but most people seemed to find one ok. Great variety of spa treatments. nan
location. shower was powerful no hanging space for more than one night

Location is fantastic, although it was pouring the two days we visited and the walk back to hotel seemed further than it was 😂   Hotel provided a cot all made up which was great.   Hotel has fridges in the room meaning we could make up and bring bottles of milk for the baby. Drinks and food are expensive. It would have been lovely to eat at the hotel but it was too much money to justify.

The room was great and the bathroom was excellent good shower and comfy bed. A few things were not available from the bar, took along time to get our cocktails, mix up with food too, things didn’t turn up what was paid for.

The hotel accommodated our daughter staying in our room at no extra charge and our room  Was upgraded. The staff were lovely and we spent our evenings in the bar which was family friendly and such a relaxing vibe. Everything was great.
The spa was lovely and the staff very friendly The food was awful we stopped having breakfast, and only ate once in the restaurant as the meal was not very well cooked

the staff were amazing, they were so welcoming and very accommodating with restaurant reservations. it was great for walking into the centre of York too. we booked three rooms as we had a group booking. one of the rooms when we checked in smelt damp and was a little off putting. we had to sleep with the windows open to try and clear the smell. luckily it did.

Good value for money. Nice environment. Available staff. Comfortable rooms. Breakfast was okay... Options in terms of fruits, yogurts, bread were variable. Rooms were clean, however the sheets were really creased....

the staff were so nice and friendly , very accommodating to my family’s needs . the establishment was clean and tidy also was very happy with the amount of entertainment there was for the kids. definitely be coming back what a wonderful time we had 10/10 nothing was brilliant
Modern, clean, good location Mattress on bed had a slant to it so tended to roll towards centre of bed.

The hotel was lovely the buffet dinner was really nice and had plenty to eat at a great price. I didn't like having to ask what was vegetarian on the dishes it would have been nice to have some indication.
The hotel was very clean and in a fabulous location. The outside pods were great. Lovely hotel. nan
close to the airport with free airport shuttle No good food options close by

Moxy was in a great location and central to where my trip was based. Local restaurants only a walk away and great facilities and food at the hotel also. Staff were polite and welcoming. Parking next door was super too - they validate your ticket! The room was great, and exactly what was needed for the short stay. The only qualm I would have was that it was not all clean, surfaces were dirty and the remote for the TV was covered in a white substance that we washed off. Perhaps a weird thing to complain about also, but the room was roasting - no matter what we did it continues to stay that way. I shouldn’t complain in the horrible Scottish weather though, just uncomfortable to sleep in.

Lovely location even though I missed the entrance first time when driving. Nice and close to the city centre and blown away with the room (141) which epitomises the whole brand. Definitely booking again for next year and cannot thank the staff enough. First class from start to finish. One happy customer. Nothing to be fair.
Great location for the rugby but not too far to walk to town.  Quirky features & comfy room nan
Warm, close to ski area, great pool It’s a bit tatty and worn out tbh. But we are there for location and price so all good. Some staff are super some are not unfortunately.

Hotel decor was beautiful very clean and spacious   Beds were comfy and huge   Bathroom was out of this world 50.00 pre authorised payment I think is pointless it’s not even a damage deposit it’s in case you want to spend anything at the bar or room service
Large bedroom, lovely view, excellent location. Pillows were rock hard, breakfast a little expensive.

The reception staff is very cool! They offered dinner good options when we arrived. Their english breakfast is AMAZING! We celebrated my husbands bday there and when we arrived to the room there was a beautiful cake! Personally I didnt dislike anything, I would potentially add a coffee machine since I love to have a espresso in the morning while still in bed.

Lovely comfortable room.  Downstairs area so relaxing and chilled! Perfectly situated for the Hydro even though that's not the reason we stayed! Nice touch giving a free drink.  Hopefully will stay again. nan

The hotel location is superb and walking distance from tube station and other facilities. The breakfast was awesome and the staff were courteous and professional. Room space was small and not much room to navigate. No seat to relax.

Soft play, swimming - things to do for kids Really like the foyer lounge area, very rustic & homely It is abit run down, there was a massive damp patch in the ceiling in the main area on the way to our room, which was roped off with buckets under it as the water dripped, for the full 3 days we were there. There were worn away areas of the shower/bath in the room? bathroom door was cracked. Just needed some general TLC.  Soft play was closed mon/Tues/Wed - which was most of our stay, that was abit inconvenient.  We stayed for 3 nights and no cleaner cleaned the room in that time. I wasn’t looking for a full clean but just the bins emptied & some fresh towels would have been nice.
Great location, clean and friendly staff. nan
Lovely and grand with high ceilings and big rooms. Slightly run down feel - cracks in the wash basin for example
Very friendly staff - we had a number of breakfasts - including Vegan option - and found the kitchen staff particularly helpfull. Good family break. nan

We stayed here 2 weekends in a row!  loved the vibes at the Coylumbridge has, cosy foyer are with little fires, good seating and a bar area. we enjoyed live music between the woodshed and the foyer. Kids arent permitted in the woodshed after 8pm but are welcome in the foyer. We loved the fun house, our little one was three and there is a little amusement section along with a soft play American diner and mini golf! You are literally 5 mins drive from Aviemore Centre & 10 mins to Loch Morlich 15 to the Cairngorms ski resort! we are returning again at Easter. check in is a bit slow after a long drive, not great when arriving late.

Nice room. Clean bar the pillows to hard -but overall  no fault here. You can hear the rumblings on the tube from underneath as hotel is next door to the Tube Station (and i mean next door) Check in for Radisson Edwardian standard not good at all.  Very drab lady checked me in, no smile whatsoever, very programatic.  It wasn't just myself, the same thing happened to a guy who was trying to laugh with her when i walked back downstairs but the computer that controls the supposed smiley face just wasn't playing ball - he quickly understood he was going to get the same check-in experience as me.  When you pay £240 for 1 night, you need a smile.  Further and more concerning on check in (at 6pm please note the time) - the first thing i was told was "your room is not ready" - i questioned this and was then found a room.  Ridiculous.  I had to twice ask what the lady was saying as she was giving no eye contact and muttering?  Terrible experience.

Location, decor, shower was very nice The pillows. We only had 1 pillow each and they weren’t very fluffy. Other than that was very comfy. However I think if the pillows were comfier we would have had a better sleep resulting in later check out but woke up with bad back. Mattress was comfy however pillows ruined it :(
Good location, comfortable rooms, helpful staff. nan

Great location, easily accessible from Amsterdam and 10 minutes away from Schipol airport. They offer a free shuttle bus to the airport every 20 minutes. It's a great option if you have an early morning flight and you want to save some time or make sure you are arriving in time at the airport. nan
location is nearly airport,  shuttlebus, personal, clean.. nan

well presented hotel, clean, very helpful staff and the premium room was excellent, good gym. Good location right next to Warren Street tube station the superior single rooms are way too small and breakfast is over priced
The hotel was clean and comfortable especially as its an old hotel There was nothing I disliked

Friendly staff - Bar staff did an amazing job with cocktails. Room was lovely and bed very comfy .
everything, it was a fantastic place to stay - very clean & great location We loved the property

Loved the location, the very friendly staff, and the bathroom. Also the family room had a dividing wall giving a bit of privacy to both bed-containing halves - and each half had it's own tv. All were happy Nothing really!
Location was perfect an the room was clean nice big fluffy towels an really comfy bed nan
Only stayed 1 night. Great vibe and bar prices were decent. Staff were great, nothing was a problem. Paying £151 when it goes down to £85 when there's not a concert on next door.

The location was great and it was a very clean hotel, the gardens were lovely too, especially of an evening when all the lights came on The room, although beautifully decorated, was quite dark, even with all lights on and the bathroom had a terrible musty smell
Brilliant location, close to attractions and shops. Dated bathroom, not easy to find hotel room, but got there in the end

Good location to York Centre. Clean bedroom and bathroom. Comfy bed Had the room right next to reception which was quite noisy. It was also over the car park which again was noisy. Clean and decorated well but few tired bits in the bedroom (threadbare carpet etc). Heating pipe ran across the room. Tripped over in the night. Feel this room should have been discounted further than the other rooms.
Spacious rooms, renovated, nice bathroom, comfortable bed, mini fridge. Expensive

I absolutely can't agree with the statement that the staff is "unfriendly". Whether it were the receptionists, the cleaning staff or the shuttle driver: They've all been very friendly, polite, dedicated and ready to help during our stay.  The hotel itself is compact but cozy. Something I found particularly remarkable was the rooms' sound insulation: The loud and busy outer area ceased to nothing but white noise once the windows were closed. When we returned in the evening, our beds were neatly made and the towels exchanged with fresh ones, which was a nice touch as well.  Breakfast costs 10€ which is a fair price considering the buffet is pretty good and varied in terms of the available options.  Although the hotel is roughly fifteen kilometers away from Amsterdam Centraal, you can get there (and back) rather effortlessly for the shuttles driving between hotel and airport as well as the trains connecting Schriphol with the central station, leave multiple times per hour.  Unlike the regular ibis hotel, it also wasn't overly crowded nor hectic most of the time which I appreciated a lot.  Unfortunately, there's not too much to see in the area, in case you like to go out and explore. But there's a McDonalds as well as a bus terminal nearby and a lot of greenery around which makes it all somewhat scenic. I didn't like the pillows since they were small and not very comfy.  It was also a little strange that shower and sink had been part of the main room.  Unlike the soundproof windows, the inner walls were rather thin, making you hear clearly what was happening on the hallway outside.
Location and fabulous breakfasts and friendly staff. It was a bit hot in the room, no air-conditioner. Otherwise all good.
The location, design features within the hotel, smart TV and the comfy beds. No storage within the rooms. No towel hooks in the bathroom for wet towels.

The location was great, close to the museum, minster and city centre. Everything was within walking distance. The room was small but well equipped and the bathroom superb.  We had breakfast, there was a good choice and it was served promptly. It was very good value for money and the staff were extremely friendly and helpful It is difficult to find fault, the only thing that made us comment was that it was very noisy in the bar when we had a drink before leaving for dinner in the evening. There were a lot of staff talking and the radio contact between the restaurant and kitchen was loud! However this is not a complaint, it was the only thing that we noticed and commented upon
Location  Priced ok for what you get nan

Good location  Modern and cosy decor Cosy relaxing bar Pizzas were delicious  Lovely touches in the room i.e. nice toiletries Room was on the small side but well desigbed considering. To be expected in a listed building

Location was good breakfast very poor cooked breakfast was dry overcooked and felt like it had been sitting in the kitchen for ages Breakfast was very poor especially the hot cooked breakfast that seems to be mass produced, dry burnt and hard to eat , presentation was poor

The property was in a great location.  It was quirky and very clean very lovely hotel.  The room was exceptionally clean, beautiful bathroom we loved the shower the bed was comfortable so big my partner loved the bed so much, best sleep we’ve both had. really can not fault anything also the bar was lush. The staff were lovely so accommodating and polite.  Well Done. loved everything.
the hotel was really clean and worth it to try to stay there ,especially the hotel have shuttle Bus from Airport and to the Airport on the early morning,it was really helpful. nan

Location great. Good WiFi and great breakfast options. Lovely staff at breakfast and reception. Can be very noisy from bar music on friday Saturday night. Parking was a problem until we found out about paying extra for booked parking spot across road ($15 pn)

the room was quirky layout and spacious  the bar and lounge area fabulous food could have been a wee bit better
