Lovely hotel in a great location. Room was very generous in size and clean. The breakfast was well organised and lots of choice. nan

The location is obviously a big win, and the cost compared to other Cairngorm locations So first of all, really thin walls and the dogs in the room 2 doors down could be heard barking continuously anytime someone walked down the corridor. I love dogs, but after a long day on the hills you want some quiet. Then the heater stopped working - not ideal for November in the cairngorms! Located a mains powered oil heater in the cupboard, so popped this on for a bit before bed. Woke up twice through the night again - once with the dogs barking in the room next door and then second time roasting hot because the heating came on throughout the night. Was disappointed that you have to book the swimming pool in advance as by the time we checked in, got showered and changed out of our hill clothes, the leisure desk reception staff were gone.
The location was fabulous, rooms clean and staff friendly. Breakfast wasn't great for the price.  Only ate there once and then ate out the rest of the time.
Cleanliness and reliable shuttle services. nan

I enjoyed an excellent breakfast. The other guests seemed to be old like me so it was a peaceful and friendly affair..  It was marvellous to have a corner bath that I could get in and out of.  I am pleased I asked for a sea view as it was fascinating to look out on the extensive pleasure facilities. There were no leaflets to let me know what I could visit e.g.. The museum, art centre, theatres for example and the receptionists weren't  clued up on such things. Apart from an advertising map for the town centre there was no map of the wider area. I needed to locate a cemetery and the reception staff could not show me, though they did suggest buses I could take.
Convenient facilities, parking area, pool and gym nan
Location, clean comfort Nothing

Room was nice. Bar area very pleasant. Excellent location. Attentive staff No WiFi in first room. Had to be moved. Breakfast first day very poor, long wait for awful quality cooked breakfast , long wait for hot drink Needed formal complaint which was dealt with well. Second day breakfast complete transformation  , although did not try cooked breakfast again due to very poor quality of first one but service so much better.
Very comfortable rooms and bed. Nice facilities A very big hotel so lots of people around

It's the Comfiest bed I've ever stayed in.  The hotel was less than a 10 min straight walk into the town centre.  Staff were amazingly friendly, and the room was top quality. Very clean and decor was modern.  We would 100% stay here again when visiting York. nan

Beautiful hotel with spa facilities.  Good location, ten minutes walk into Bath.  Beautiful room & grounds. Room wasn’t cleaned adequately.  Hairs on bed, cocktail stick on floor, shower in bath didn’t drain properly.
Friendly personnel, clean, good breakfast, great location Tight room

Room was adequate but nothing great. Good buffet breakfast. Useless receptionist who gave us the wrong directions to our room resulting in us climbing four flights of stairs for nothing. The same receptionist also only programmed our keys for only one night meaning we had to queue up at reception to get them programmed again.

The staff at reception were exceptional, very friendly and helpful during check-in. The room itself was of a high standard for the comfort and amenities provided. Great value for money. Will definitely be returning. nan
Super clean, great amenities, professional friendly helpful staff , good rates N/a
Cleanliness, Facilities, Location, Staff, Ambiance Breakfast options could be increased
amazing place to stay, staff were lovely and kind, the room was clean and so beautiful. the beer garden was amazing and great to sit outside, couldn’t fault a thing. nothing.

Great location for Euston station. Clean room.  Friendly staff. Yummy breakfast. Rambling collection of buildings glued together to be one large hotel makes for long walks from the lifts. But that's only a problem if you have a lot of luggage, or you're really hungry for breakfast!  Also, don't expect a nice view if you have an interior window, so request a room that looks "out" to a street.
Very nice building, full of character.  Easy walk to the centre of York, and quite good parking. Expensive
clean, comfortable and well located. value for money, great breakfast nan

The location of the hotel is excellent for anyone attending events at the Exhibition Centres (or exploring Glasgow). The rooms were spotless, really fresh with modern decor. We had a great nights sleep. We were blown away by the choice and variety of breakfast, lots of fresh produce which was all delicious.   We had dinner at the hotel, the menu was limited but the food was great, sometimes keeping things simple equates to higher quality. Hard to think of something! The window overlooked the car park but we appreciated it is located in a built up area - on the plus side we didn’t heard any noise from the busy road.
Room was very good lots of room for myself and the grandchildren.  Good variety of food for breakfast nan
Staff was very friendly and helpful. Breakfast was very tasty. The room was comfortable and clean. Quite noisy on Saturday evening with local clubs.

Hotel room was clean and functional.     Breakfast menu and Evening menu was excellent and very good quality Car parking as not great. Struggled to enter the car park as there were so many larger vehicles parked by the entrance. It was also awkward leaving the car park after our stay.    Hotel room teas and coffees needed to be refilled every morning, rather than requesting these at the reception desk. This should be automatically checked on a daily basis.
Location was perfect and staff very friendly and helpful. Breakfast was delicious and a great choice of menu nan
Really modern, clean hotel, rooms lovely and bed so comfortable. nan
Breakfast fine Would like to have been informed in advance that breakfast would be in Ibis due to work going on at Ibis budget. Unable to cancel the pre-booked breakfast.

Excellent stay in central London with convenient transport links. The bus stops and tube station are literally at the doorsteps and the hotel is within walking distance from major shopping area and the West End. nan

Located near bus station. Early check-in. Airport shuttle.  Clean room. Breakfast good value. Rude kitchen staff.  More room needed at bottom of bed. Bed sheets small so kept untucking.  Building not aesthetically pleasing from outside.
Breakfasts good value for money Rooms very spartan
Good location for our needs. Excellent breakfast. Nice room. Pleasant staff. Needs a bit of updating. Floor in room very very creaky! Very slow & small lift
Breakfast was lovely. Great to order from menu. Location really central. Was a little noisy overnight (Saturday) with people in street.

I'd included a comment to the hotel saying I intended to do some art and arrived to find I'd got a double aspect room with lots of light - and a note from the staff. That was really lovely. The room was large and had two places for sitting - one small table and a place that was more like a mini office space with desk lamp and sockets (both standard and USB). The bed was really comfy and the TV screen was placed in the right place for viewing when in bed. Breakfast was great - it's a buffet style and there was so much to choose from. Most of the staff were very friendly and helpful. It's a very warm September and the room was too warm at night. There is air-con but it goes off after a while so I woke up in the early hours each night and had to get up to put the air-con on again. But this would probably not be such an issue if it hadn't been the hottest few days of the year! The hotel is a little way from town centre, but there are plenty of buses if you don't want to walk or get a taxi. (There's even a free bus!)

Great location right on the waterfront. We had a harbour front room with great view. Older style hotel but all the mod cons necessary. Breakfast was provided in the downstairs restaurant and it was superb, great choice and fresh and tasty. Trendy bar downstairs with happy hour and good Tassie wines. There was a step down from the bathroom to the bedroom which was something to be aware of in the dark.

The staff were very helpful and the breakfast was great value Not having access to our room before 4 We had rode up on our bikes for the thunder in the glen rally through rain and hale arriving at 2 and had to wait until 4 to gain access. The only other niggle was we had paid for our breakfast each day and both days when we arrived we had to wait 15 minutes while the restaurant staff checked we had paid as it repeatedly said we hadn’t on their form. The staff themselves were good about it but it was still embarrassing being asked to wait in front of everyone queuing to be served.

It is a very nice modern hotel, very cozy atmosphere. Everywhere was very clean, even outside the hotel. The area and the hotel is nice and quiet Our room had a bit of a strange buzzing noise, but it wasn't too loud.

The style of the hotel was really fun and modern but with a stylish retro twist. The staff on reception and the bar were so helpful and welcoming. It really made a big difference to our stay.  The location was perfect and with a feee Hop bus Monday to Saturday made it even easier to get in to town.  The buffet breakfast was good with plenty of choice too. Nothing!
Overall superb staff … High quality food… Very helpful staff nan

Great hotel and there was a lovely group of army veterans and friends staying last night, it was a fantastic party atmosphere. Nice big room with comfy bed and sea view. Delicious breakfast. Will definitely stay again. Nothing.

Great location  Lovely spa. Staff very attentive Had a facial in the spa. Staff didn't seem to realise I was due for facial even though was pre booked and paid for. Waited for about 10minutes so allot of time slot wasted resulting in a very short expensive facial

Large hotel and at an incredible price for what it is too. The room was a decent size and was very comfortable. If you're looking at staying in the area then this has to be one of the top choices, can't go wrong for the price. The car park had limited space and was very tight, but I appreciate that you can only work with the space you have. The hotel is a little dated in places (the small rickety lift for example) but it is an old building and this is part of its charm.
Access to airport Very tight space, earwig bug parading on towel!  Additional towels would be good!
Love the location, beautiful grounds and good spa facilities All good

It was one of the cheapest options available, whilst still having decent transport links to Amsterdam city centre. Although the room was small, it was perfectly functional to stay at for a night It was a very compact room therefore the shower and toilet are very small spaces. The beds were not the most comfortable and the pillows were especially uncomfortable.
A wonderful hotel that conveys the style of the city. Comfortable and cozy. The kids pool and spa was great The bath in my bathroom was not really clean... But no worries;)

This hotel is in a great location in central London. The deluxe single room was bigger than most single rooms I’ve stayed in. The staff were all really friendly. It was clean. Lovely toiletries. Nice bar area. Noisy.
Spa facilities are excellent and staff were very welcoming Food was very average and overpriced
Room was warm and cosy . The meals were fantastic both at dinner and breakfast. Everything was in walking distance that we wanted Nothing!

The location was ideal,The room we got is better for for a single traveller, not comfortable for two people to share the right space. But a great value for money these days. Too small for 2 people to share. A right squeeze bit we made it work..
Vicinity to Euston. Plenty of places to eat. Lovely area Wifi a bit patchy
the location and the breakfast is very good.Room size very large if you have a street front room the noise below on a weekend can be a bit distracting

The location is exceptional. Service and included breakfast were great too. Would definitely recommend to anyone who is travelling to Hobart. The building is old the corridors are a weird shape, but that doesn't really matter
The location was excellent. Staff were very accommodating. Great value for money No food available maybr because out of seaaon

The hotel itself is stunning on first view. the drive up the hotel with the gardens are beautiful. very well kept and full of its original features. staff couldn’t be more helpful. The heating in the room wasn’t working and the bath was a bit grubby. also found a few items from the last occupant. The spa facilities i.e men’s and women’s changing rooms were not clean.

Beautiful old hotel. Staff were lovely and went out of their way to help with us with places to see in town. Bed was comfortable. We didn't eat at the hotel so unable to rate the food. Water in shower took a while to heat up but then was ok.
location,clean and comfortable noisy room as our room located next to the doors from lift lobby. the doors banged closed and also the ice machine clatters through the night. that needs relocating
the spa and hotel grounds was lovely. the staff friendly and helpful. short staffed at breakfast, very long wait for service and food. bed was two single beds pushed together and was very lumpy
fabulous location, we could see the beach from our room. spacious bedroom and bathroom, the room was spotlessly clean too. the pool facilities were available during our stay which was disappointing.

Stylish hotel, very nice room and trendy bar and restaurant, friendly staff (mostly).  Easy walk to De Montfort Hall or into town, 15-20 minutes. Overall we enjoyed our stay; we booked breakfast when we got there but this didn't seem to have been passed on to the breakfast supervisor, so we had a 5 minute discussion before he let us in,   Parking is very limited at the hotel and there's not much in the immediate area, especially on match days. We checked in and dropped off our luggage, then parked in the East Street car park for £11.95 for 24 hours.
Staff excellent. Breakfast & evening buffet excellent. Facilities for families/children very good. Relaxed and friendly atmosphere. Bathroom tired.
The decor was lovely and modern, so so clean. The bedrooms were very modern and fresh and the bed was incredible! The lift sometimes didnt work and kept saying ‘call locked’
Close to the center only 30 minutes with bus. The bus station is just across from the hotel. The room was pretty small.
Good value for money . Great location nan

Amazing hotel.  Staff are really friendly and helpful.  The hotel is really clean and up to a fantastic standard.  Facilities in the room and great.  The bar has a good selection of drinks and cocktails.  The pizza hatch outside the hotel is a great bonus. -

Waterfront location is handy for shops and restaurants.  Spacious room.  Very friendly and helpful staff. you No parking.  And confusing and difficult to find. Wound up with $45 parking ticket as we stayed in one spot for 1 hr 15 min although set for one hour only.   A little bit of warren to wind back to rooms with short stairs to navigate with luggage.
Staying in Glasgow for a concert so hotel was in an excellent location for the hydro! Hotel was very welcoming. No complaints! nan
Close to the airport, comfortable and in case you need it, you can purchase breakfast. nan
Large to in room good shower Not a big selection on cooked breakfast,quality so so very pricey downstairs £1.70 for a bounty.

Location was great only 5 minute walk and right in the centre of town. Room very small, and very hot alright for a few nights but wouldn't want to stay for a week.  Plus £15 per night for car parking a bit steep especially as car park is on their land.
cleanliness, convenience, politeness of staff and price nan

The location is excellent with a great restaurant as well. The room we stayed in overlooked the gardens and was a huge king room. The bed needs to be upgraded it had no support.   The room is very lit up at night due to street lighting.
Staff was very welcoming, the location was great, everything was clean Really really minor: the fact that they didn’t have electricity near the bed

Breakfast provided was very good. Lots of fresh options for the continental Only blinds in the room so could see in when opened in morning as no barrier if anyone else looking through their window. Questionable cleanliness as there was a half eaten cake in the fridge from a previous customer.
The hotel was fab. The only thing that let it down was the lack of swimming pool opening times and the slide being closed for the kids. A sauna and steam room would be great
Perfect location with amazing views lovely hotel with spacious room very comfortable bed Nothing
Perfectly adequate for a one night stay. The bar and restaurant at the pricier Ibis hotel are a short walk away. It's only "budget" compared to the cost of other hotels in the area.
Was exactly what I needed.  comfy bed, clean room and good shower. nan

Location was amazing for  having concert in near-by Armadillo, took stress out of leaving early or running for train. Bar open until 2am which means night continued after concert. Very quiet in rooms. Large, clean bathrooms. Good hot & cold breakfast selection.  Hotel entrance and bar very quirks and modern, whole music vibe! Bed was just a tad too soft for me but some people like that. Could do with a veggie sausage for breakfast even if you need to order in advance would be worth it otherwise £15 a bit much. In saying that for meat eaters there was a lot.
Great location. Comfortable room. Lovely food. nan
Great location and most staff were extremely friendly. Very good breakfast and meals in the restaurant. A couple of staff members were not as friendly as the majority of the staff.

great family hotel, good facilities for kids, breakfast was first class it’s a bit dated but it’s no big deal, it’s a family focused hotel that offers great value, it would be better if you could charge drinks to your room at the restaurant
Fantastic staff, but £15 for breakfast was a little steep nan

very nice hotel if a  little shabby (bathroom in need of some painting), very nice garden. we had the opportunity to sit outside in the evening and thoroughly enjoyed it. We got a free upgrade to a very comfortable and nicely decorated room. complimentary bathrobes and slippers. breakfast! unfriendly member of staff, buffet choice was fair but no choice for bread and you are brought 2 slices per person and have to ask for more and wait for it to be toasted. not sure it was worth the price (20£) so we had breakfast in town the second day.   the Spa definitely needs some renovations! broken tiles in the pools!!

The staff were super friendly and helpful, and made my daughter and myself feel welcome. We were meant to stay in a cozy bedroom but were upgraded to a family room upon arrival. Breakfast was also delicious. Nothing

great staff.  really nice friendly cool people.  very accommodating and kind.  great location and was absolutely immaculate.  probably the best hotel i ever stayed in.  beautiful rooms great food and bar.  perfect.  thanks my stay was a joy 🤩 just wouldn’t know what to put here
Very central location easy access to tubes Northman and Victoria line , Oxford street , Soho and Embankment all very close on tubes. Nothing it was all good

Location was excellent with a 10 min walk into York. Staff extremely welcoming and friendly. Beautiful gardens and outdoor area to sit and eat in. Small and uncomfortably hot room even with fan made it difficult to sleep along with a hard mattress.
restaurant staff were lovely especially the greeter  female ✔️. bar staff good . rooms far too hot . good not bad for price. rooms adequate. hot rooms , bit shabby,
Modern design, clean and comfortable. Fairly long walk to get groceries.

The rooms where great and the bed so spacious and comfortable. Great location a short distance from town. The food in the restaurant and breakfast was perfect with a great variety. I visit many hotels and will definitely stay again. The price could be a little cheaper

The breakfast was very varied, from lots of fresh fruit, to fresh waffles, to a full english fry up. Everyone was catered for: both the gym buddies and the gluttons. Being asked to pay £50 for a deposit.   Being told to pay £20 for an early check in.

Perfect location for access to the Cairngorm hills. Was upgraded to a more spacious room when I arrived. Didn't have breakfast so can't comment but the room and bathroom were spacious, modern and clean. Great staff and lots of facilities in the hotel.  For the price, excellent and wouldn't hesitate to return. Nothing.
Location was great.  Breakfast was fantastic. As it is an old building just be aware you may need to navigate some stairs to get to your room.  Staff did offer to assist with bags.
good breakfeast and room , location all good. all good
Great location if you are going to watch Leicester Tigers   Nice surroundings and food choices, the service and staff were very good. nan
Excellent breakfast excellent location Tv too small

It was very reasonable and the room allocated a good size for us and the two dogs ! Room had I think been recently renovated. Location good , close to the sea front and town to the rear! Lots of dog friendly places near ! Front and interior slightly dates and in need of updating but what you might expect for such a hotel! Not that it was that bad .

It is a great American style hotel. Very clean and comfortable. The facilities in the bedroom were exceptional. I have paid more and got a lot less. The room had everything you could ask for: books to read; writing pad; fridge; nice wall mounted TV, etc. Rather too impersonal for my liking and quite a long walk out of the city centre. One particular thing they don't tell you before you book is they charge a deposit, so I needed to photograph any damage when I arrived to make sure I wasn't charged for something that had been damaged before I arrived.

Suite was very nice. Bar and lounge seating area very comfortable and lots of space. Heating in the bedroom was off so very cold at night. Background music in the lounge bar area far to loud, most of the residents were aged over 60. We had to move to the foyer area to converse. That was our biggest issue. Lounge music should be background not disco volume. A fundamental error for a otherwise nice hotel.
Breakfast is lovely and so was the room the bath the bed and everything you get xx Tv didn't have Netflix or YouTube ect but let's you stream from your phone  Bath at side let water out
Excellent Spa and massage Expensive breakfast so declined
Room was well equiped, modern and clean. Excellent location. Bed and pillows were hard

The reception/bar staff were very friendly and helpful. The room was basic but nice and clean and everything worked. Soaps were a lovely scent. Considering it was next to a busy road and I was closest to the bar, it was very quiet. Fantastic to be able to get to the airport on bus 'A' almost right outside the hotel and being so close helped with any anxiety about getting there on time. Upon arrival l thought the entrance was a pub so didn't immediately go there. Was a bit weird to go to the bar to sign in, wasn't clear at all and after a long journey I just wanted to check in. Breakfast was a bit disappointing, cereal was stale and coffee was a bit 😕.
city centre location perfect for a short stay  for shopping and the theatre clean smart decor friendly staff
Great location for public transport and shops. Lifts, one out of order.

The decor is beautiful and everything is so clean.  The room was spacious and the shower was fantastic.  The fact you can connect Bluetooth to the TVs and play audio from your phone is really helpful if you need white noise or something similar to sleep. Even so close to the train station and city centre there was very little noise and sleep soundly all night. The lifts are a little slow sometimes but that’s me being picky.

The room was large and nicely designed and furnished.  I'd booked a large room - but that turned out to be right beside the elevator, I was nervous about noise - but slept peacefully,    Lovely bar and access to the Train station. nothing
I didn’t have breakfast. LOVED the free shuttle to and from airport. The location and view was great with nice restaurants in the area.  Hotel was clean. Enjoyed lunch and drinks at the cafe. Nothing
Central location. Very comfortable bed. nan
Comfy, firm beds, great shower, good-sized room. nan

Location is perfect to walk around city centre. elegant and classy hotel, very courteous and helpful staff, buffet english breakfast. We were given initially a large room with a cousy toilet and bath. but it was located at the back, window view only the side street.  We were granted a change of room with the front city view but the room has no phone to use to contact the reception (rm. 720). Still, quite a big room and comfy bed with large screen tv.

My third visit to the beautiful grand dame of hotels. Service on arrival and departure at reception was first class. Requested a high floor, was granted a spacious quiet Classic Double on the 8th floor. Well laid out, lovely fixtures and fittings, the all important chiller and a very comfy bed. I enjoyed coffees and cake in the stylish lounge by reception. I will be back, hopefully for a birthday visit! Only thing i missed was the full length mirror for selfies!

The location, the first impression, the food, most of the team were friendly, welcoming and efficient, the room was exceptional, the Beatles artefacts and pictures, the bar area was very comfortable and the prices were very reasonable. The outside of the hotel was spoiled by the litter and rubbish in the corners at ground level, the room was not ready at 3.30 and despite being told it would be just 10 minutes and they would come and find us we had to go back to reception after 30mins to find out what was happening. We were not the only ones. The hotel presentation is very grand and luxurious as it should be for a high calibre brand but some of the staff uniforms were grubby and shabby and took away from what should be a very professional presentation. Most of the staff themselves were very friendly and helpful but just let down by their connectedness to the overall presentation.
Well located. Decorated nicely, room spacious and comfortable Really noisy with guests coming back. Long after midnight and corridor noise horrendous. Fortunately, didn’t last too long
Comfortable beds and brilliant views. Staff were friendly. More knowledge of the area and public transport would be helpful.

Shuttle from airport was easy to find, right on time. A great service. The next morning it was a short walk to the ferry. Pleasant staff. The breakfast buffet is very good and a good value. Avra perfectly suited our needs. nan
We loved the massive comfy beds and breakfast was fantastic Poor view from bedroom window but didn't bother us

The hotel was excellent very clean with friendly and knowledgeable staff. We ordered the free shuttle to and from Athens Airport which was on time and made our transfer from airport to hotel and vice versa effortless very please Thanks all. nan
The ambience, the location and helpful service especially the front desk. The breakfast was excellent. Some BBC programmes were unavailable at the time of our visit.

Good location near tube. Extremely clean and new accommodation. It's a little pricey but reasonable given the high costs in London. Early check-in option would have been nice if you are traveling from USA with time change and no sleep. I would have paid extra for this option
The shuttle service is very convenient. The bed sheet is a bit dirty.
Great views of the harbour. Nice and quiet. Well specced room. nan
It was close to a lot of café’s and restaurants, the ocean and walkways. The parking was smelling and the drive ways needs a pressure hose cleaning.
position if the property, handy to tram line, supermarket if not wanting to eat out, but also,plenty of restaurants in the vicinity. nothing

One Bedroom Ocean room lovely on 45th floor. View was exceptional. Great location with a light rail stop a very short walk from hotel. Good restaurants near as is a supermarket The matresses on the king bed were extremely hard. The hotel needs to put a mattress topper of some sort on the beds. My wife ended up sleeping on the sofa as the bed was so hard.

Avra hotel is definitely the best place you are going to stay if you have to take a ferry or a flight the next day as it is really just around 3 minutes walk from the Rafina port and a free shuttle bus distance from the airport. I can see that that's the reason why so many people are staying at Avra hotel. Apart from that, Avra hotel is facing directly east, which gives you an astounding sun rise view (even if your room is not facing east, you can go down to the restaurant and enjoy your free breakfast with that breathtaking sunrise). Not to mention Rafina has some really good and affordable restaurants (with views of course!). A bit surprising that there was no kettle in the room

I’ve stayed in so many hotels in leeds for business and this is the best by far. Great shower, steam free mirror, nice breakfast, lovely staff, excellent lighting in room. Really comfy. Will be the only hotel I stay in for work now. Nothing

Location was fab. Room was lovely and so were the staff. Bed was nice and comfy and we got some free chocolate on arrival!  The hotel bar was lovely too. Nothing really, suppose the shower could've been better, but that's the only point I would mention.

Great central facility that was very clean & unit had everything needed for a short stay.  Having an onsite restaurant that is open quite late is also awesome & the food was really nice. No complaints from me
Great value, location and staff. Couldn’t have been more impressed. Would have been nice to have bottled or filtered water available in the room.
Fantastic stay. Central to shops, bars etc. Very clean. nan
Very friendly staff and easy check in. The location was efficient because it’s located within town centre therefore we were in walking distance of many restaurants and attractions. nan

Size of room &the extra bed/bedroom we had as it was unexpected. Comfortable. Shower pressure was amazing. We were only there 2 nights &it was nice to go out for the day &housekeeping changed out our towels & refilled our toilet paper & took our rubbish. Only thing we didnt like was the slow wifi & the fact it kept kicking us off.

We have found that the Avra Hotel at Rafina is a wonderful location to relax before our onward journey.  We always stay here when moving around Greece. The free shuttle to and from the airport is a brilliant service.  Friendly efficient staff, spotlessly clean modern rooms, lovely sea views and a wide choice of breakfast. Excellent value for money! nan
The hotel is located beside the beach and jetty. You can see a beautiful sun set. Restaurants are just around the corner. The basin is not inside the toilet.
Weve stayed here multiple times Love how clean and comfortable the hotel is The staff are always polite and helpful Whole general feel of the place is warm and welcoming. nan

Loved the location, it was easy to tube everywhere. The staff were friendly and there was no issues checking in or getting to the room. The room itself was really clean and well kept with the usual tea and coffee making facilities. The shower was great and more powerful than I was expected. Nothing much, the shower rail had broken so I couldn’t adjust the height but it was already pretty high up to begin with.

Overall a gold hotel right next to the airport. There’s a bar and a restaurant which is open till the evening, so that’s nice. Considering the location, the price is fair. It’s walking distance to the terminal. The room was enough for us and it was clean. Be careful! The sockets are only UK standard (which makes sense as the hotel is in UK), so you will need an adaptor in case your device is EU standard.   I tried contacting “24/7 support number” both by calling them and texting in WhatsApp, no one picked up the phone and my message was ignored (even though it was read). That spoiled the whole impression.   Also, the flow of water in the shower was weak.
Easy, clean, good location. No frills but comfortable. Loved the fact they had plunger coffee available! nan
Location fabulous, hotel very good, nice room, nice breakfast. At check I feel a little information on the hotel would have been nice, i.e the bars, the restaurant. Check in felt like a formality.
good value for money all good
Location great. Breakfast bit chaotic was much better when self service. What was a nice restaurant is now like a canteen with sauce bottles on tables. As above.

Love the location, enjoy the fab views we experience from our room. One other thing we noticed on the first night when it was windy - the wind was coming in the left side of the window.  It was not a deal breaker.
Close to airport Tiny old rooms

Beautiful hotel, friendly helpful staff. Nice and clean everywhere and breakfast had a good choice. Would suggest having another milk station as had to queue up to get milk for cereal with people wanting a hot drink nan

The breakfast was plentiful and enjoyable. The bed was very comfortable and the room, very clean. The room window was open on our arrival and the smell of street food was very strong. Although this was resolved when we closed the window
Spacious rooms with great facilities. Just a few steps away from St Enoch centre and central station but quiet too. nan

Our booking was for 9 people from 3 families with adult children. We booked 3 x Classic rooms with balconies and 2 x Twin Rooms. All rooms were great but we all preferred the Classic with balconies. lovely view, clean facility, great location, wonderful staff. Meriton southport has been the best stay!! nan
location was good  beds were comfortable  place was clean not applicable
Friendly good value and clean.Friendly staff and good restaurant Nothingh

This must be first choice for travellers. My flights late night OR take off early morning. Night arrival and bouncy blond receptionist nothing too much. Freezing outside. Hot tomato soup quickly warmed me up. While everything is 'basic', its all there. Electric appliances, special (and extra if required) bedding. King size bath tub to submerge into, and soak away the grime of a hard days work in London. 10 minutes walk (briskly) to airport, but by the time to go you are well fortified with a good solid breakfast. If the eggs are 'cool' and have stood too long, ask for fresh. This is a budget hotel not the Ritz but they DO try to give you the service of a 5* hotel. Its only a tiny reception and half the day no one is checking in so if you arrive and there's a queue of 5 or more, chill, drop your luggage, have a beer (or a tomato soup) 😄  you won't find a better home from home good times and difficult times. Thank you Ibis. I once had a major problem. They used to have a left luggage (no more) and I couldn't get back for nearly a year when I normally stayed every 6 weeks. On my return to left luggage I reached for the door handle and...... I just found a brick wall! In my absence the hotel had been restructured my clothes leaving with the old brickwork! I managed to list my winter clothes and produce some receipts. Embarrassment all round me for leaving belongings so long, the hotel for not checking thrown aways more carefully. In the end a most generous cash and credit combination and as this was many years ago I must have stayed more than 50 nights since 🙂 Happiness for everyone.

Restaurant for breakfast and dinner. Everything ok.

For a hostel, it’s a little bit expensive.

breakfast is great.   location is also great. The only thing I was not happy with is that there was an old used towel in the bathroom (probably forgotten to be removed). Everything else was excellent.

Varied breakfast, wide choice, good products: this is what you can expect of a 5 star hotel, even though it was pretty crowded and packed but this is understandable for such a touristic city like Prague.  Perfectly located right in the city center, everything accessible by walking, and the valet parking service for my private car was fast and professional (I could pick it up quickly at 2am). Our room was quite small (which can have its charm!), but what makes it annoying is that the soundproofing is pretty bad. You can hear people talking in the corridor, so much that the hotel displays signs everywhere asking guests to be quiet between 10pm and 7am (experienced in both rooms 219 and 710). I am lowering the score for that reason, as this is not up to the standards of a 5 star hotel with such a recent built year (2006 with renovation in 2019).

Everything. The location, price, quality of the room and bed. One of the best me and my wife have stayed in. Although a microwave would be nice to have in the room. Everything else no fault. We will definitely be coming back here nan
location and the size of the room late check in and no housekeeping for short period

The location was perfect as we wanted to stay in the cbd. The facilities were great and the room is exactly the same as the pictures. This is my only fault and it’s more a personal choice.

Location .near some lovely bars and restaurants. Staff were very friendly and helpful Everything is done electronically from check in to check out.....there is a control panel  next to one side of the bed that controls everything  from lights to blinds. This is convenient until you need to use the bathroom at night and can't turn on the light as there is no switch at the bathroom door and you risk turning everything on with the control panel... There is also no physical lock on the main door this is also done electronically  by key card ...I didn't like this at all ...it felt unsafe....what is wrong with a regular light switch and lock..I don't know .. There is also nowhere to put cosmetics toothbrush etc as there is no shelf in the bathroom.. The beds are way to high and are on a sliding  system that separates  into 2 singles as needed. We had booked a twin which resulted in one of the beds being pushed up against the window which didn't close properly so there was a cold draft all night. .there was a little shelf between the narrow single beds but it was too low to use without risking falling out of the bed. These apartments  would work very well as a double but not as a twin and I am sure that other people would love them ..they are just not for me

There was a cleaning issue in my room when I checked in, which was a bit unpleasant. However, it was quickly sorted out by staff. I found the AC to be very uncomfortable as it was creating a strong draft right onto the bed, even on the lowest possible step. I had to switch it off during the night, which I guess would have been a problem in summer. The bed was quite hard, but still ok, but the duvet was like a heavy brick, as were the cushions. Usually you would find at least two types of cushions (high/low, hard/soft) in a hotel at that price. The toiletries are really cheap, no conditioner or body lotion. Bring your own. This hostel is in the city-center, but close to a police station so it felt very safe. The check-in process was very easy and they provided clear instructions on how to access the room. There are a number of codes you will need to to access the elevator and the room itself, so I found it to be very secure. You can also download an app to give you contactless entry, which was even more convenient. My room was very clean and had large locker for my suitcase. The washroom was very clean, and the shower was hot. The lounge and restaurant were pretty busy but I did not spend too much time there. I was lucky enough to share the room with quiet and respectful guests. The staff were very helpful as well. I arrived early and asked to check-in my bags, and they had a room with lockers, but they were all full. Not their fault, just a heads up to expect you might have to leave it in a room that's not perfectly secure. The room did get a bit warm at night, but we kept the window slightly open for air circulation and that was fine. My only major complaint was that I had to shorten my stay two weeks before my check-in date from 3 to 2 days, but they did not honor the original rate, so I nearly paid the same amount for 2 days as I would have for the 3...

The customer service was phenomenal, I had one minor issue and my entire stay was heavily compensated. Honestly I had forgotten there was ever even a problem by the time I had left.     Friendly staff, fantastic food, FULL and modern gym equipment (which is truly underrated, it’s a fantastic feature to offer). Honestly I cannot find complaint. Maybe longer / 24hr room service, or perhaps a more developed executive lounge. It was nice, but it was still restricted to some degrees.     It could have used a TV and maybe other amenities to make it more like a lounge and a bit more “executive” but this is definitely nitpicking. The hotel is still 10/10.

Friendly staff, clean bed, and nice bathroom. Located in the centre and close to the bus station. However, it was completely dark in the room and the sensor of the indicator light under the bed is not sensitive. I needed the torch from the phone to see things, hope the sensitivity of the light sensor could be improved. But overall is a nice experience, I believe if I could arrive earlier I would have liked this place more.

Well what can I say about this place one of the coolest places I've stayed in the pods are something else it's like your own little cubby house with your own little curtains for privacy very friendly staff situated only minutes away from the shops it really is the perfect place those who'd rather not pay expensive hotel prices I would most certainly recommend this place to anyone and I plan to return in the near future it exceeded my expectations This is only a minor thing hence why I didn't give 10/10 the food could have done with being a bit warmer but other than that can't fault this place

Room was a little too warm, bed and pillow can be more comfortable.

The hotel was quite possibly the best we've stayed in, very comfortable bed, great breakfast and lovely helpful staff. Can't think of one thing I didn't like.

Our initial room was on the ground floor.  Although there was nothing wrong with the room, we decided we preferred a room on an upper floor as there was a lift.  When approachd, reception said "no problem" and offered us a choice of 2 rooms, one of which had a sea view, which we accepted gratefully for the payment of a small premium.  The breakfast was excellent and the dining staff exceelent. We had not anticipated that there would not be a bus from the station to the hotel.  We walked down, me rather slowly due to a mobility problem, which took time as it was raining.  This of course was really down to ourselves as we had not done our homework beforehand.

I loved my river view. The room was spacious, well appointed and clean. The bathroom and toiletries were lovely. Good room service breakfast and a really great location. This is very minor, but I felt I was being chased out as my room door was knocked five minutes before checkout time(I as putting my jacket on). Seems unreasonable for such an expensive hotel.

Represents great value for money as only a fraction more than some of the tired old motels on the way to the airport. Couldn’t get my phone to cast Netflix / Kayo to TV but that’s pretty minor quibble.

Great location.  10 minutes to the metro station.  Lots of bars, restaurants, and theaters in the area. The room is very compact but workable for 2 people.  I'd suggest opting for a bigger room if staying more than 1 night.

Excellent location. Comfortable room . Very good breakfasts although the service on Sunday was chaotic in terms of availability of food and staffing. Very difficult to get decaf coffee at breakfast. Machines offered endless combinations of coffees but if you wanted decaf make it  yourself out of a Nescafé jar.

The room was clean and quiet. Location was great. No Wi-Fi available in the room. Reception was not friendly on arrival, pedantic about cards although I had paid in advance in full. The breakfast was bland.

City hotel totally exceeded our expectations. We booked the comfort room with the balcony and its definitely worth the extra money. We had a stunning view of the castle at sunset. Check in and check out were very easy. The bed was comfortable and room was very big in general. High end soap products in the bathroom were a nice touch. The bath and shower were very clean. The breakfast was fantastic, huge selection of hot and cold options with local fresh produce too. The offer a pillow menu, have a small fitness center and a nice terrace area. Not really a fault of the hotel, but there were a lot of bugs in our room from outside despite keeping the door to the balcony closed. There's a lot of mosquitos in general in the area, but there were a lot of insects (moths, flies and mosquitoes) on the walls in the room when we arrived.   The aircon isn't super powerful which makes it a little hot in summer nights.
Facilities were comfortable and having restuarant on site was handy when arriving overnight for an ongoing journey nan

Wonderful staff, incredible decor and very comfortable room plus much needed “late” checkout time. Hotel is also in an incredible location for Southbank activities and a short walk from Waterloo A couple more english breakfast teas in the room!
